## Hidden Halal Restaurants Beijing: Niujie Beef Noodles, Hopson One Fried Chicken and Local Hot Pot
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 78d609dd5abd6e4552b91b101646dad6dc422fe8e50627db4f812ef52f758aff

# Hidden Halal Restaurants Beijing: Niujie Beef Noodles, Hopson One Fried Chicken and Local Hot Pot

Reposted from the web

Summary: This hidden halal restaurants guide for Beijing covers issue 33, featuring Hopson One halal fried chicken, Niujie beef noodles, Xingyuexuan hot pot, local barbecue, and practical restaurant notes for Muslim diners.

Beijing Halal Food Map (33) is presented here as a firsthand travel account in clear English, beginning with this scene: In the days right after Eid al-Fitr, I was still getting used to eating during the day and my appetite was much smaller. The account keeps its focus on Beijing Halal Food, Muslim Travel, Middle Eastern Food while preserving the names, places, food, and historical details from the Chinese source.

In the days right after Eid al-Fitr, I was still getting used to eating during the day and my appetite was much smaller. My weight dropped below 70kg and hasn't bounced back. Actually, it is quite good to get into the habit of eating only two meals a day. Skipping lunch helps me stay clear-headed and awake in the afternoon.

1. Chicken Tender Master (Jiliu Daren)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ni1y4q.jpg)

Chicken Tender Master is a chain brand, but only this Hopson One (Hesheng Hui) branch is guaranteed to be halal. The owner is a friend (dosti) from Tongliao who studied in Egypt, and his parents are both Hajjis. So, please make sure to choose the Hopson One branch when eating at Chicken Tender Master. I suggested to the owner that he place a subtle halal sign in front of the register to make it easier for friends (dosti) to confirm.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/izz65a.jpg)

Jiuxing Chicken is a very reliable halal food factory in Hebei, and many friends (dosti) have said it is trustworthy.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/l1xgy4.jpg)

They only sell three things: chicken tenders, rice cakes (niangao), and french fries. They guarantee fresh oil every day, so the fried food comes out golden and very fresh.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/og7w3f.jpg)

The fried rice cakes puff up and are hollow inside. The seasoning powder sprinkled on top is a secret recipe and the highlight of this shop.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1zvujk.jpg)

The owner says this fried food is best eaten right out of the fryer. He does not recommend takeout because it affects the texture. The freshly fried chicken tenders are crispy and delicious, and the rice cakes are crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, which really satisfies a craving. This kind of small stall does not involve selling alcohol, so the money earned is clean and wholesome.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bx5s4b.jpg)

The address is Shop 168, 21 Block, Basement Level 2, Hopson One.

2. Wei's Big Bowl Noodles (Weiji Dawanmian)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/c1xrso.jpg)

Wei's is a new small beef noodle shop on Zaolin Front Street in Niujie. They have grilled skewers, stir-fried dishes, and grilled fish.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y20g3d.jpg)

We didn't get to eat the grilled fish that day, so we just ordered a bowl of beef noodles. This Beijing-style beef noodle soup is what we call soup-poured beef noodles (jiaotang niuroumian). It is just like Chai-style beef noodles, except Chai-style sells the noodles and beef separately.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6eifsn.jpg)

Small skewers on bamboo sticks are also a traditional Beijing-style barbecue. The skewers tasted average, so I still prefer the beef noodles.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/z1976b.jpg)

3. Xingyuexuan Four Seasons Hot Pot

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/281wt9.jpg)

Xingyuexuan used to be Liankexuan on Niujie Street. It has a new owner now, the same person who owns the Lan Dayouxian beef and mutton shop on Shuru Hutong. Since the change in ownership, the meat quality has improved. The meat quality at the old Liankexuan was not good.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/chcq3d.jpg)

Lan Da's meat is high quality and all comes from Inner Mongolia. This shop hasn't been open long, so you usually don't have to wait in line. If you can't wait at Jubaoyuan, you can come here instead; the taste is about the same.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/e6kyfw.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k89h2f.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mijknz.jpg)

A highlight of Xingyuexuan is that they sell Haagen-Dazs ice cream for about 30 yuan a serving. It happened to be Eid al-Fitr that day, and the manager gave it to us for free.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0hr699.jpg)

4. Tianqiao Lao Jin Hot Pot

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yfeo2r.jpg)

This is a branch of Tianqiao Lao Jin located on Jiaozihutong in Niujie. They spent a long time preparing, and it finally opened during Ramadan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8xtyx6.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6s1mym.jpg)

They focus on large, affordable portions. The mutton has a strong gamey flavor, and it lacks the refinement of a big restaurant, but the prices are cheap.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7icrjh.jpg)

There is no fancy plating; you just eat for the rustic experience.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/avmcki.jpg)

The chess-piece-shaped flatbread (qizi shaobing) is also very ordinary. Their specialty is a dipping sauce with vinegar. I guess most people aren't used to such sour sauce, so you can also choose the non-sour sesame paste dipping sauce.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g3yn8w.jpg)

4. Jingyi Farmhouse

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g984nd.jpg)

Jingyi Farmhouse is a courtyard run by Teacher Chen Hui from the former Halal Bookstore. It covers several acres at the foot of the mountain near Xiguanshi Village in Changping. The yard is full of fruit trees, and it is filled with birdsong and flowers when you visit in April.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g61qy0.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m7raxt.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1xg5pt.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ft9eds.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nugllw.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ej8ww9.jpg)

The yard has chickens and sheep raised in a completely natural, eco-friendly way. You can book ahead if you want to eat here. We held our Eid al-Fitr gathering here this year, with over forty people meeting at the farmhouse to barbecue, drink tea, and chat under the open sky.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tbry7o.jpg)

Teacher Chen's wife prepared Xinjiang-style noodle soup (fentang) and rice pilaf (zhuafan) for us, along with fresh fruit and vegetable salad.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1z3czi.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vbrdxm.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ezm5ml.jpg)

We cooked over a fire in the yard. Food cooked with firewood has a down-to-earth, delicious flavor.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zwgkgu.jpg)

With over forty young people, two big pots of rice were not enough, so we started an outdoor barbecue.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ztzwg6.jpg)

The grilled fish was very popular. The smoky flavor from charcoal grilling cannot be matched by electric grills.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/j9j25x.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dxxrwp.jpg)

I usually do the grilling at our events, and the skewers are snatched up as soon as they are ready. The mountain behind me is Fushan Cemetery. The grave of the famous writer Wang Xiaobo is in the new section eight of Fushan Cemetery. Anyone who likes literature will know Wang Xiaobo.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/uslqnm.jpg)

5. Ruizi Dumpling Restaurant

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/iu85hp.jpg)

At Ruizi Dumpling Restaurant in Xiguanshi, Changping, the signature dish is dumplings, but I think their steamed dumplings (shaomai) and meat pies (roubing) are even better.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/uefjqs.jpg)

We came at noon and it was very crowded. Everything with a filling is wrapped to order, so the wait is a bit long.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/iwr7mt.jpg)

The taste did not disappoint, especially the beef steamed dumplings and the meat pie. Their meat pie is huge, just like a Shandong thick pancake (zhuangmo). Two people definitely cannot finish it; one pie is enough for four people, so be careful with how much you order.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2bxod2.jpg)

The dumplings taste good too. They have many kinds of fillings, and I love the beef and green onion ones.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/n40rx3.jpg)

6. Beizi shop at Xiguanshi Market

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f01s40.jpg)

In the Xiguanshi market, we found a baked flatbread (beizi) shop that has been open for many years. We asked the owner, and he is indeed from Hohhot. This baked flatbread is a specialty of Hohhot and is also found in Baotou. It is a food unique to Hui Muslims.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4s5kxj.jpg)

We bought a few sugar-filled baked flatbreads (beizi) to go. They also have savory baked flatbreads and sesame flatbreads (shaobing), and they fry dough fritters (youxiang) every day at 4:00 PM. Baked flatbreads (beizi) and flatbreads (nang) are staple foods that are easy for Muslims to carry when traveling.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0oaneo.jpg)

We found a few unique small shops in Xiguanshi village. We did not have time to eat there, but we look forward to trying them one by one next time.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vae4nk.jpg)

This flatbread (nang) shop is great. It is run by a Uyghur young man. He only sells flatbreads and nothing else. We have bought from there twice.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ex94wv.jpg)

Jia County buckwheat noodles (heluo mian) from Henan

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3xrtwo.jpg)

Hai Family beef covered pancakes (niurou zhaobing) in Baoding

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wirlna.jpg)

Clam vermicelli (huajia fen)

8. Maidebao (Niujie branch)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/kuegkc.jpg)

Maidebao opened another branch on Niujie. This shop used to be called Lebangke, and the address still shows Lebangke, but it has actually changed to Maidebao. It is an alcohol-free fast-food restaurant, suitable for couples on dates.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g4x6mu.jpg)

The Niujie branch features many Islamic design elements.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gjhogo.jpg)

Arabic calligraphy calendars hang on the walls.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hg68lu.jpg)

The tables hold Arabic novels and tea from Zhengxingde.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nmmudt.jpg)

Small Islamic-themed ornaments sit on the dining tables.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/08s2rf.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/w9jf46.jpg)

The burger menu now includes a double beef patty option.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/71grpb.jpg)

Besides burgers, they offer a variety of pizzas.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/umjayu.jpg)

I think the pizza tastes better. I had the Margherita pizza this time and will try other flavors next time. Their french fries and chicken nuggets are also delicious.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2fv2nc.jpg)

Margherita pizza

9. Pakistan Truck Art Festival

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/smg7l4.jpg)

The Embassy of Pakistan in China is hosting a truck art festival at the Silk Road International Arts Center in Langfang from April 28 to July 24.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/rctasf.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yoxa5c.jpg)

Truck art is a traditional part of Pakistani culture. These beautiful hand-painted truck bodies were created by the Pakistani truck art group Phool Patti.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yeconj.jpg)

The Phool Patti group commercialized truck art, which helps protect this cultural heritage while creating income for local artisans.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jycm79.jpg)

Truck art started as a traditional painting style used on horse harnesses and carriages. As industrial transport grew across the country, many types of imported trucks came into use. During this time, artists and truck owners began putting a lot of effort into decorating these new vehicles. These carefully decorated trucks drove into remote areas and quickly became eye-catching symbols on the road.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bgpkdb.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/eqwpxl.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/27imjl.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zb5n4u.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/j9n0fn.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xjfao6.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gbpqkn.jpg)

There was a free buffet on the opening day, but I am not sure if they will have it later.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0o2t1b.jpg)

I had dinner with a beautiful Pakistani host.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/n8q1ot.jpg)

The big festival cake was cut into small pieces and eaten up in an instant.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/b25pif.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mucfxp.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mpwjl9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9ykm8l.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/755wtu.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gu4yjm.jpg)

The main courses and dessert tables were set up on both sides of the exhibition hall. This was my first time eating these cold cheese sweet rice balls (tangyuan), and I really liked them.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ffvm42.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/412o6h.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/o9x0wk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/c0k2f1.jpg)

We took a bus from the Pakistani embassy to get there and back. The bus broke down on the highway on the way over, but it did not affect the mood of our Pakistani friends at all. Everyone kept laughing and talking. Luckily, we were not late for the opening ceremony, and the event started on time.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ry2xxf.jpg)

## Muslim Travel Guide UK: Oxford, Cambridge, London Halal Restaurants and Islamic History
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 8d6ef9682f537e8b773904dc4923d087dab8138303860ba4a1baed45a36eb731

# Muslim Travel Guide UK: Oxford, Cambridge, London Halal Restaurants and Islamic History

Reposted from the web

Summary: This UK Muslim travel guide begins with an unexpected London stopover, halal food at Gatwick Airport, Crawley Islamic Centre, London halal restaurants, city walks, Muslim street life, Hyde Park, Paddington, and the route toward Oxford and Cambridge.

I originally planned to go to Canada, but 6 hours after taking off from Rome, the flight had an emergency and had to turn back. The plane flew over the Atlantic for 3 hours before heading back to land in London, which unexpectedly gave me a 7-day visa-free trip to London.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cw3yfq.jpg)

A friendly reminder: Chinese passport holders with Canadian or American visas can enter the UK visa-free when transiting, but you need to have an onward ticket. I tested this myself when flying from Canada back to China with a layover in London. I arrived and departed from two different airports. The check-in staff in Canada asked if I had a 24-hour departure ticket from the UK. My flight arrived in London on the first morning and left on the second evening, which was over 24 hours, but I was still allowed to board. When I entered London, they gave me a 3-day stamp, so to be safe, you can stay in London visa-free for at least one day.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/d1qdvd.jpg)

I actually visited London twice. The first trip was a total accident, and the second was a layover on my way back to China. The flight transiting through Europe was almost entirely white passengers. When they heard about the emergency landing in London, not one person complained; they actually looked happy. The flight attendant even joked that we would have another chance to eat the great British dish fish and chips (zha yu shu tiao). This is a running joke, as fish and chips is a traditional British food, and it mocks the UK for being a culinary desert.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/w066a2.jpg)

I just walked around and found two halal restaurants at London Gatwick Airport.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5mgxa6.jpg)

Diners lined up at this stall to buy fast food, and the airline gave every passenger a 15-pound voucher as compensation.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/v3lw3k.jpg)

15 pounds is about enough for one fast-food meal.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ck57j4.jpg)

This is a London flatbread wrap with vegetables, and you can choose whatever ingredients you want inside.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/90hqkx.jpg)

Prices in London are higher than in Canada. The exchange rate for the British pound to the Chinese yuan is 1:9, which is much higher than the 1:5.3 rate for the Canadian dollar. Standard hotel rooms cost over 1,000 yuan per night, so the accommodation provided by the airline felt like a win. After returning to China, I also claimed 1,200 yuan in insurance compensation, which basically gave me a free three-day trip to London.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2gy5g3.jpg)

After leaving Gatwick Airport, you can walk 1.5 kilometers to the small town of Crawley. The greenery along the road is beautiful.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/h5tcwx.jpg)

Seeing this star and crescent symbol felt quite familiar, but Premier Inn is actually just a budget hotel chain.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/azt0n0.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pcp4ye.jpg)

Crawley Islamic Centre & Masjid.

This is an Islamic school and mosque owned by people of Pakistani descent. The status of our 'iron brothers' from Pakistan in the UK is unique. First, most hold British passports. As a former British colony, Pakistan has a special, easier path for immigration to the UK. Currently, people of Pakistani descent make up 3.3% of London's population, and there were 1.6 million Pakistanis in the UK in 2021, with 20% of them living in London.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qg6jqn.jpg)

Pakistanis in the UK also have strong economic power. They work not only in catering but also as teachers, doctors, and engineers. The unemployment rate for Pakistanis in London is lower than the average for the white population. Additionally, one in seven Pakistanis works as a taxi driver.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8og6j5.jpg)

I estimate that this small town of Crawley is a Pakistani enclave. The vast majority of women I saw on the street were wearing headscarves.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y0jyw0.jpg)

The history of Pakistani immigration to the UK dates back to the 17th century. At that time, the East India Company brought a large number of Indian and Pakistani laborers to the British Isles. These early immigrants were almost all men, and many married white women in the UK.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/w4rwpf.jpg)

Muhammad Ali Jinnah, the founding father of Pakistan, studied in the UK before returning to Pakistan. After Pakistan gained independence from India, more and more Pakistanis immigrated to the UK.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ok4nep.jpg)

Many elites of Pakistani descent are involved in British politics. London Mayor Sadiq Khan, Manchester Mayor Yasmin Dar, Birmingham Mayor Chaudhry Abdul Rashid, Newham Mayor Rokhsana Fiaz, and Kingston upon Thames Mayor Shiraz Mirza are all British citizens of Pakistani descent.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pu0jat.jpg)

The streets of the small town are clean and beautiful, with flowers planted in front of every house. The temperature in London in June is cool and the sun is bright. People say London is foggy all year round, but unfortunately, it was sunny both times I passed through, so I missed the fog.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/11pw8z.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tgn1qk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bvgtj1.jpg)

A family has a Palestinian flag hanging in their window.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qj7c10.jpg)

There are many mosques around the small town of Crawley. This one is the Ahmadiyya mosque. I wrote about the Ahmadiyya in detail in my article 'Goodbye Vancouver: A Colorful Journey of the Soul,' noting that it is a sect not recognized by mainstream Islam.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xsk5i2.jpg)

The name above is the fifth Caliph of the Ahmadiyya, Mirza Masroor Ahmad. He currently lives in London, and the Ahmadiyya built the largest mosque in the UK.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k67znp.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/heyxjk.jpg)

If you see a mosque named Noor in Europe or America, be aware that it might be Ahmadiyya.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/79e469.jpg)

Another Sri Lankan mosque is less than 800 meters away from the Ahmadiyya mosque.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/s32ra2.jpg)

The gate was locked when I arrived, but I saw someone cleaning inside and asked him to open it for me. He was the one who told me this is a Sri Lankan mosque.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ak2gpz.jpg)

We can see from the scale of the Sri Lankan mosque that it is clearly not as grand as the Pakistani mosque.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/x4bqsm.jpg)

Besides the Sri Lankan mosque, London also has Bangladeshi and Indian mosques, which look about the same from the outside.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/q4z6yg.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/26bin0.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vmnke3.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ys0w0w.jpg)

The highlight of the Sri Lankan mosque is that it sits right next to a church, and everyone lives together in harmony.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jlmx5o.jpg)

I strolled back from the mosque and walked to the town's commercial center.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wkqnav.jpg)

I saw a halal supermarket, just like the ones on Ox Street (Niujie).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/t9qxcb.jpg)

You multiply all the price tags by 9 to get the price in RMB; life in London is not easy.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/25yoz3.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/618fcn.jpg)

There are also some imported goods from Muslim countries. The customers in the supermarket are dressed in all sorts of ways, but most are in Muslim attire.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/smhmee.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qboabo.jpg)

People eat shrimp, crab, and other seafood here. Although Pakistanis also follow the Hanafi school, the Hanafi school does not completely forbid eating shrimp and crab. For details, see how the Hanafi school views shrimp.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ppnu6h.jpg)

There are non-alcoholic drinks, including brands like Coca-Cola and Pepsi that are supposedly controlled by Jewish capital. I have even seen brands like Starbucks and McDonald's around Mecca. You could say the world economy is integrated, and none of us can escape this economic system.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ancxki.jpg)

Seeing the wildflowers by the road, I felt like I was in Xinjiang as I walked. Britain is in the northwest of Europe, where people are tall and the climate is on the cold side.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7rldrv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/u9wwxw.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/68c2b9.jpg)

The British Museum

I had plenty of time for this trip, so I arrived at the British Museum early to visit. The museum is free, though you can choose to make a voluntary donation. You need to book your visit on the official website in advance. Tickets are easy to get, and I definitely chose the free option.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qyh9e4.jpg)

The exterior of the British Museum is modeled after the Parthenon of ancient Greece. Moving from left to right, it represents the river of time, showing humanity moving from ignorance toward science. In the center, the goddess of wisdom holds a golden scepter with a golden globe at her feet, representing the arrival of the god of science. Sitting next to the goddess is the god of mathematics.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/oxn4j8.jpg)

There is a line to get in, and you have to go through a security check before entering. The inside is huge, and it takes about a full day to see everything properly.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/47bg5v.jpg)

The British Museum is open from 10:00 to 17:00, and stays open until 20:30 on Fridays. The Ancient Egypt gallery is definitely the most popular.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bvcfoa.jpg)

Some people online say that after seeing the ancient Egyptian artifacts at the British Museum, they thought Egypt had been emptied out. I have been to the Egyptian Museum myself, and there are so many artifacts there that there is nowhere to put them all, so it is impossible that it was emptied. See the Egypt travel guide for avoiding pitfalls for more details.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9ytjil.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9uszsp.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bysyn9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ayfotl.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9r2l3z.jpg)

Rosetta Stone

Made in 196 BC, it is inscribed with a decree from the coronation of the ancient Egyptian King Ptolemy V. The stone has three types of writing: Ancient Greek, Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs, and Demotic script. Archaeologists were able to decipher the content of Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs by comparing the three scripts, because Ancient Greek can still be read today.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0azvrg.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/u674ub.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/w69b3v.jpg)

Nereid Monument

This is a monument for the ruler of Xanthos in southwestern Turkey, built between 390 and 380 BC. The Nereids were the daughters of the sea god. In 1840, the British Museum funded archaeological excavations in southwestern Turkey with the permission of the Ottoman Sultan and discovered this temple. At that time, the Ottoman Empire allowed British, French, and German archaeologists to conduct research. It was not until after 1869 that the export of artifacts was prohibited, which led to the later construction of the Istanbul Archaeology Museums.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ylsiq1.jpg)

The mysterious Moai statues of Easter Island

In 1868, the British soldier Richard Powell brought two Moai statues from Easter Island back to the UK and gave them to Queen Victoria as a gift. In recent years, Chile has repeatedly asked the British Museum to return the statues, but the British side has not responded.

Islamic World Exhibition

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y7onch.jpg)

Islamic World Exhibition

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fbm2qv.jpg)

This monogram (tughra) was a common symbol in the Ottoman Empire, and the name on the gold coin is a person's name.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f8dqj8.jpg)

This tells the history after the birth of the Prophet Muhammad.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/kjal0v.jpg)

This describes Jerusalem, the holy city shared by Judaism, Christianity, and Islam, as well as the story of the Prophet Muhammad's Night Journey and Ascension. The Dome of the Rock (Qubbat al-Sakhrah) is connected to this event.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jt9mop.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8rklsd.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/e3nnzk.jpg)

This is a lamp from a mosque, related to the Ottoman Sultan's order to renovate the Dome of the Rock. It is inscribed with a Hadith that compares believers in the mosque to fish in water and non-believers to birds in a cage.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4pmzxs.jpg)

A compass used to determine the direction of prayer (qibla).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f5o0uk.jpg)

This piece of black volcanic stone may have once been embedded in the wall of the Kaaba.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gp7a54.jpg)

The picture above shows a bronze mirror. Inspired by China, medieval Iran also made mirrors out of copper alloy that could reflect light after being polished. The back of the mirror is engraved with patterns and served as an amulet.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bvhvn3.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vcm5se.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/78x0hv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qbii9p.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qeozfc.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mr94gu.jpg)

Asia: The Legacy of Mongolia

The image above states that the Mongol invasion of West Asia was led by Hulagu, the grandson of Genghis Khan (1162-1227). Hulagu founded the Ilkhanate. He spared the lives of the West Asian rebel forces because their specialized skills could be put to good use in the new Mongol power centers.

The Mongol legacy continued through the Central Asian leader Timur. Timur claimed a connection between Genghis Khan and the Prophet Muhammad. During his conquests, Timur also kept craftsmen and brought them to his new centers of Samarkand and Herat. He blended Iranian Islamic and Central Asian steppe traditions into his architectural art.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3y3bjd.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3grclv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/66h6mp.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0ztx4m.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tekqmd.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vftwts.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m5df68.jpg)

Regarding building materials from Iran and Central Asia, ornate glazed tiles were widely used in religious buildings and homes. These bricks may have come from a mosque and are inscribed in Arabic with 'In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious' and 'There is no god but Allah, He is the All-Powerful, the All-Knowing.'

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/n13wbk.jpg)

This is a tombstone for an Ottoman naval officer named Seyyid Ahmed Bey Kor Kasos. He served the Ottoman Empire for 60 years and passed away during the Greek War of Independence. The stone records his battles against the Greeks during the Greek War of Independence (1821-1832).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bqdeu8.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sgy5t8.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mmyqjg.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1qzxoi.jpg)

Ottoman Turkish women's slippers and children's slippers.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a299u1.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fcmw6u.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ckw7nh.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8xgj8j.jpg)

Iran and Central Asia.

The Safavid dynasty took power in 1501 and established the first Shia state in Tabriz. In 1598, under the rule of Shah Abbas I, the capital moved to Isfahan. Safavid art became popular within the empire and spread to Europe and East Asia. Armenians involved in global trade helped help this cultural exchange.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qj5bxm.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/i7hppp.jpg)

Islam in Africa. Islam has been present in Africa since the early 600s. Today, about half of Africa's population follows Islam. North, West, and East Africa have long been centers of the wider Islamic world. The writing board in the image above was mainly used to teach Arabic and the Quran.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4ay3la.jpg)

A hat worn by an official from southern or central Nigeria. They may have been Hausa clerks employed by British colonial institutions.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1mfjd7.jpg)

China Gallery.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/b5avme.jpg)

The Chinese artifact gallery at the British Museum is quite large. It is divided into an artifact gallery and a jewelry gallery. The people who spend the most time here are Chinese. The British Museum holds about 23,000 Chinese artifacts, ranging from Shang and Zhou dynasty bronzes to Tang and Song dynasty ceramics, and gold and jade items from the Ming and Qing dynasties. More than 2,000 of these are on long-term display.

Chinese civilization is one of the oldest in the world. Today, China is about the same size as Europe and has one-quarter of the world's population. The China gallery displays 7,000 years of history, divided into different periods.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/knsw5h.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hhsatb.jpg)

Decorative glazed tile walls on the roof ridges of temples in Shanxi Province.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xly4nh.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wf5j1j.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pj7v7t.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vukv1k.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xake5y.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a5mmr2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/43to48.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yvkgwq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/i6x31u.jpg)

You can find information about the British Museum's collection online, so I did not take photos of every single item. Many people are now calling for the museum to return artifacts to other countries, but whether they can be reclaimed depends on national diplomatic strength.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0ev08b.jpg)

I am actually puzzled by why the British were able to recognize the value of these artifacts over 200 years ago, while other countries at the time saw them as junk and let them be taken away. If the British Museum had not collected them, I fear a significant portion of these artifacts would not exist today.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/r37i4i.jpg)

After leaving the British Museum, I saw this Uyghur restaurant nearby. The waiter is from Kashgar and has lived in London for over 20 years. He was very friendly, though he does not speak Chinese. I even left my luggage at his shop.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/93tc24.jpg)

At this shop, I ate the most expensive hand-pulled noodles (latiaozi) of my life, costing 13.95 pounds, or about 130 yuan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zp8cwy.jpg)

The taste was very authentic, more so than the two Xinjiang restaurants I visited in Canada.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vmhc1x.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0davz1.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ufe1l8.jpg)

Lamb skewers (yangrouchuan) are 3 pounds each, with a minimum order of two, which is cheaper than the cold dishes.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sxg9lc.jpg)

After the meal, the waiter quietly gave me a pudding. It was delicious, and the whole dining experience was very pleasant.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gijt2o.jpg)

After eating, I walked back to my hotel. Everywhere I looked along the way, I saw Muslims. It is no exaggeration to say that the proportion of Muslims on the streets of London has already exceeded one-tenth.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ftjzb8.jpg)

A Muslim couple sunbathing in Hyde Park.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/apto81.jpg)

London is a city with beautiful street views. Its buildings have a deeper sense of history than those in Canada, making it perfect for a slow city walk.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/izn5wb.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/r6hxsv.jpg)

Paddington Street near Hyde Park is lined with halal shops. From Paddington Station, you can take the subway to the central station and transfer to a train for Cambridge or Oxford, reaching your destination in about an hour.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y3pr7l.jpg)

Cambridge

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xuhdbc.jpg)

Cambridge street view

---

## Best Halal Food Kuala Lumpur: Pizza Hut, Nasi Lemak, Indian Meals, Thai Food and Dragon-i
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 10fdb1213adeff467b0e4e9fdf9b7afcff01f37957b0cc33788473977c520df2

# Best Halal Food Kuala Lumpur: Pizza Hut, Nasi Lemak, Indian Meals, Thai Food and Dragon-i

Reposted from the web

Summary: This Kuala Lumpur halal food guide issue 4 continues the city series with airport Pizza Hut, nasi lemak, Indian meals, beef roti, Thai food, Japanese supermarket snacks, coffee, yong tau foo, Italian food, Dragon-i, and practical halal dining notes around KLCC and the airport.

Since my son started kindergarten in Kuala Lumpur, I have to travel back and forth between Beijing and Kuala Lumpur. Because of this, my Kuala Lumpur Halal Food Series will keep updating.

Kuala Lumpur Halal Food Map (Part 2)

Kuala Lumpur Halal Food Map (Part 3)

The restaurant information for this issue is as follows:

1. Pizza Hut (American-style pizza fast food)

2. Nasi Lemak Royale Kedah (Malay coconut milk rice)

3. Amman Mess (Indian main meal)

4. Beef Roti (beef flatbread)

5. Thai Cuisine (Thai food)

6. Don Don Donki (Japanese supermarket)

7. Jardin Coffee (coffee and desserts)

8. Yong Tau Foo (spicy hot pot)

9. NATALINA ITALIAN KITCHEN (Italian food)

10. Dragon-i (Chinese food)

1. Pizza Hut (American-style pizza fast food)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ztpomb.jpg)

Just like in Singapore, international fast-food chains in Kuala Lumpur are 100% halal. Some people ask why I didn't list McDonald's or KFC. It's because those shops are everywhere, so I don't need to recommend them. However, these American brands, especially Starbucks, don't do very well in Kuala Lumpur. They face widespread boycotts in the Islamic world due to their Jewish background and public support for the LGBT community.

I personally don't boycott any products, but I don't oppose those who do. I just feel that boycotting is a bit like a child throwing a tantrum and rarely achieves much. First, most employees of these companies in Islamic countries are Muslims, so you end up hurting your own people while trying to hurt the enemy. Second, the global economy is interconnected. Even without considering that boycotts don't have much effect, there are always ways to bypass economic sanctions imposed by the U.S. I think it is better to improve our own skills and defeat our opponents with real strength.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/i0owy5.jpg)

This is the Pizza Hut in the international departure dining area of the airport. Their pizzas are baked to order and take about 10 minutes. The fresh pizza is delicious and the price is not expensive. Keep in mind that at Kuala Lumpur airport, you go through security right before boarding, not before entering the gate area. You also cannot bring water through security, so leave enough time to walk to your gate to avoid missing your flight.

2. Nasi Lemak Royale Kedah (Malay coconut milk rice)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3c5zgw.jpg)

I have been to this shop so many times that I pass by almost every day to buy a glass of fresh fruit juice. A glass only costs 6 RM, while it would cost at least 20 back home.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mclzav.jpg)

The staff here are almost all of South Asian descent and know me well. Even though their wages aren't high, they live happily every day. They greet me when we meet and love to joke around. This optimistic personality is easy to catch.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mvsgvt.jpg)

This Pakistani brother can speak a little Chinese, and when he serves my food, he always saves the biggest shrimp for me.

Address: Ground floor shop at Wisma Centre.

3. Amman Mess (Indian main meal)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/czwahf.jpg)

This is probably the most Indian-style restaurant I have ever visited. It is located on Little India street near Kuala Lumpur Sentral. This street has a strong Indian vibe, is quite clean, and you can find all kinds of Indian snacks here.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ou69gj.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mjxegy.jpg)

The restaurant displays photos of the Malaysian royal family and is halal certified.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a8g7ix.jpg)

All the servers are Indian, and they wear the traditional red dot on their foreheads.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/31qadt.jpg)

If you are familiar with Western society, you will have a lot of respect for Indian people. They often do better abroad than Chinese people. Many top companies in the U.S. have Indian CEOs, which is something worth thinking about.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/uapfdc.jpg)

There was a pot on the table. To avoid any misunderstanding, I specifically asked the server if the water inside was for washing hands, and I learned that it was drinking water.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/krpq7t.jpg)

They serve Indian milk tea and curry. One server walks around with a curry pot, specifically to add curry dipping sauce for diners. The food is served on banana leaves. Most of the customers here are Indian, and they eat directly with their hands.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0wc4v0.jpg)

There is a slight difference between Indian food and Pakistani food. Indian food is spicier, while some Pakistani food incorporates characteristics of Arab cuisine.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ycv5xq.jpg)

I specifically ordered this curry shrimp dish, which was very spicy. I mentioned in a previous post that it is very common for Hanafi Muslims in South Asia to eat shrimp. This was confirmed in Malaysia, as every Indian restaurant I have seen in Malaysia sells shrimp. See details in: How do Hanafis view shrimp?

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/e22c4i.jpg)

After the meal, I strolled over to Little India street to buy some crispy snacks called panipuri. These are street snacks and are not sold inside the restaurant.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1qaze2.jpg)

Address: The ground-floor shops on one side of the large archway at the entrance to Little India.

4. Beef Roti (beef flatbread)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dk7m5j.jpg)

There is a beef pancake (niuroubing) shop in the food court on the third floor of NU Sentral Shopping Mall near the central station. It sells the kind of beef pancakes you find in Northwest China for 6 RM each, and there is often a long line.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sgvpla.jpg)

Address: The food court on the third floor of NU Sentral Shopping Mall.

5. Thai Cuisine (Thai food)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8lgscb.jpg)

Next to the beef pancake shop is a Thai food stall. Thai food feels a bit fresher than Malay food.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m8jtnb.jpg)

I ordered a beef fried rice and tom yum soup, and both were quite tasty.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/12v09y.jpg)

This mall is a few hundred meters from Little India and close to the train station, making the area great for walking around.

Address: The food court on the third floor of NU Sentral Shopping Mall.

6. Don Don Donki (Japanese supermarket)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qtn6ph.jpg)

The Japanese supermarket inside NU Sentral Shopping Mall has mostly halal products, and they are clearly marked on the shelves. We bought some cookies and chocolate here, which Fahim loves to eat.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6b0vns.jpg)

Many Japanese goods in Southeast Asia are halal and very popular with the locals.

Address: GF floor, NU Sentral Shopping Mall

7. Jardin Coffee (coffee and desserts)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y7rc3o.jpg)

There are many coffee shops in NU Sentral Shopping Mall, including some domestic chains, but I find this one has a better vibe, and the desserts and coffee are good too.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mcx1no.jpg)

This shop is right at the entrance of the Japanese supermarket, so it is a perfect place to sit and rest when you are tired from shopping.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dwowzb.jpg)

Address: GF floor, NU Sentral Shopping Mall

8. Yong Tau Foo (spicy hot pot)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wqom6d.jpg)

This shop is called Yongtaifeng in Chinese. It is a spicy hot pot (malatang) chain that mixes in Southeast Asian flavors. You grab a bowl, pick your own ingredients, and the staff will cut the vegetables for you, weigh them to calculate the price, cook them, and then ask which soup base you want.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/rbf39n.jpg)

I could not recognize many of the ingredients. It felt like there were a lot of soy products and gluten, but not much meat, so it leans toward vegetarian.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xlx44e.jpg)

I chose a tom yum soup base. The sour and spicy flavor is very appetizing, and I think girls who dislike greasy food will like this place.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4brjyb.jpg)

Address: Food court on the second floor of the KLCC Twin Towers

9. NATALINA ITALIAN KITCHEN (Italian food)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sm7l6d.jpg)

This is a very charming Italian restaurant located in AK Mall.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1aow8n.jpg)

I feel a sense of familiarity with Italian restaurants now because my son loves pasta. When I worry about what to feed him because he is a picky eater, the safest choice is always pasta.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8uzuzh.jpg)

He ate the whole bowl of noodles, so I didn't get a single bite and cannot comment on the taste.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/x7lppm.jpg)

This dish is called GNOCCHI TARTUFO, which you can think of as Italian potato dumplings. I originally wanted to order pizza, but the waiter misunderstood me. I decided to just go with it and try them, but the texture was sticky and I don't think many people would like it.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/57rtpk.jpg)

The octopus salad tasted pretty good, and it is certainly healthier than eating fried food.

Address: 4th Floor, AK Mall.

10. Dragon-i (Chinese food)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xbdotl.jpg)

This is a Chinese restaurant serving southern cuisine. It is positioned as a mid-to-high-end spot, specializing in various soup dumplings (tangbao) and also serving Beijing roast duck.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/d04mys.jpg)

It is also a pork-free restaurant.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yb8749.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/h1ximf.jpg)

The white fungus and poria jelly (yin'er fuling gao) is light and healthy.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8z98wq.jpg)

This bowl of noodles is called Shanghai ramen. It looks very light, and it tastes very light too.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cwqlhy.jpg)

Their custard buns (liusha bao) and soup dumplings (tangbao) are truly delicious. The skins are thin, the fillings are generous, and the broth is savory. The taste is just as good as Din Tai Fung.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ihlfca.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pceif5.jpg)

The fried chicken cutlet served with the ramen is also delicious. This shop is worth recommending; it is not crowded and the service is great.

Address: The food court on the second floor of the KLCC Twin Towers.

---

## Where Can I Find a Halal Restaurant in Beijing: Gulan Renjia, Mosque-Area Stews & Jingnan Hotpot
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: ea21b8ac1ed8eaa6e73760bba9d7ce1da5a770c99480a062ae7c8c9902acf465

# Where Can I Find a Halal Restaurant in Beijing: Gulan Renjia, Mosque-Area Stews & Jingnan Hotpot

Reposted from the web

Summary:
 A practical Beijing halal restaurant guide answering where to find halal food, from Gulan Renjia and restaurants near mosques to stews, craft-beer-style dining notes, and Jingnan hotpot.

When I reached the 9th installment of my Beijing halal dining guide series, I thought I was near the end. But I keep finding new and interesting halal restaurants that I just have to try. Thanks to contributions from followers and recommendations from foodie friends, I have reached the 12th installment. With a list of places I still want to visit, I think I will easily reach 200 restaurants.

Restaurant 169: Gulan Renjia

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bmp3mx.jpg)

This small shop is in Doudian, Fangshan, not far from the Doudian mosque. I walked in after seeing a sign for dipping beef tripe (zhan niudu). I had this in Kaifeng, Henan, years ago and have never forgotten it.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6d6ms0.jpg)

They recently added roasted rabbit head to the menu. Rabbit meat is halal to eat. I once had halal spicy diced rabbit and Shuangliu rabbit head in Chengdu, Sichuan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qlbwp6.jpg)

Their roasted steamed bun slices (kao mantou pian) are delicious. They are toasted until very crispy and sprinkled with barbecue seasoning, making them smell and taste great.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/evk6vj.jpg)

The dipping beef tripe is tender, but the flavor is a bit mild. The clay pot also comes with vegetables for dipping, so be careful not to burn your mouth.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/rr2sua.jpg)

Address: 50 meters north of the 993 bus terminal, south of Doudian Village, west side of the road.

Restaurant 170: Zhangji Dayangshu Restaurant

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k6y5lf.jpg)

Zhangji is right across from the Doudian mosque. Many Hui Muslims in Doudian have the surname Zhang; the owner of the Tongshunzhai restaurant next door is also named Zhang. I have been here twice, and what impressed me most is how fast they serve the food.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/q3kp00.jpg)

Maybe it is because I usually order things that are already prepared, but their stir-fried dishes also come out incredibly fast. My food was ready by the time I finished washing my hands.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5x7vq1.jpg)

I recommend the mixed beef and lamb liver. They are very flavorful, and you can order a platter with a mix of different ingredients.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lmo665.jpg)

The lamb trotters are great for gnawing on and really satisfy a craving.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8kcvjy.jpg)

Address: Directly across from Doudian mosque.

Restaurant 171: Liangji Braised Noodles (huimian)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/olj35f.jpg)

My last post recommended the spicy soup (hulatang) at Mawu in Zhongguancun, and the response was huge. This time, I am recommending a place for halal braised noodles. The noodles at Liangji are very fragrant and tasty.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k7g12w.jpg)

Because it is in Huilongguan, far from the city center, the spicy soup is only 10 yuan a bowl.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8feh75.jpg)

The overall style is very Central Plains, and the shop always plays Henan opera.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tngeh1.jpg)

The pan-fried buns (shuijianbao) are not as authentic as Mawu's and cost 5 yuan each, but they taste okay. In Henan, you can get three for 1 yuan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6bxpht.jpg)

Spicy soup recipes vary wildly, and Liangji's version is not as spicy as the one at Mawu.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g7n5ou.jpg)

The braised noodles are decent, though of course they cannot compare to the ones in Zhengzhou.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mwz1uo.jpg)

Address: Unit 104, Ganglong Commercial Area, 18 Huilongguan West Street, Changping District (east side of Industrial and Commercial Bank of China) (Huilong Clearance Center).

Restaurant 172: Guan Craft Beer Restaurant

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/o4sxuj.jpg)

This place is quite unique. Although it is a bar, it serves halal food. The owner is a Hui Muslim from Beijing.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a36hca.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cbaxj2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/b4grad.jpg)

They have burgers, pizza, seafood barbecue, and cold vegetable dishes. It is a mix of Chinese and Western styles, blending local and international flavors.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/w3yjpj.jpg)

The food is quite tasty.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ds321t.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9rnhep.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3ivfxa.jpg)

Address: Courtyard 35, Huilongguan West Street.

Shop 173: Erjie Earth Pot Stew (Erjie Diguo Dun).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5e1yie.jpg)

Xueying Village in Daxing is a treasure. The area is surrounded by halal restaurants, and a common feature of halal spots in Daxing is that they have plenty of space.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sc5n6j.jpg)

Erjie Earth Pot Stew is run by a woman from Heilongjiang nicknamed Erjie. Both Erjie and her sister, Sansan, work there, and all the staff are young women from Heilongjiang.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sbb0bo.jpg)

The portion in this large iron pot is enough for 10 people.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tv4psf.jpg)

We chose a pot of beef tendon and brisket (jintou banao), and this huge pot of meat only cost 158 yuan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qjf09u.jpg)

We added some fish tofu, corn on the cob, black wood ear mushrooms, and potatoes, then let it stew and stew...

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wrb7xp.jpg)

For our staple food, we had unleavened steamed flower rolls (huajuan). We placed them right in the pot, and they were cooked in no time.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/in8uyk.jpg)

The pot was filled with a fragrant, delicious meal. Three of us ate for a long time, but the amount barely went down. The portions are truly huge, so I suggest coming with at least 5 people.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nz0f91.jpg)

Address: No. 6, North Fourth Alley, Qingren Road, Daxing District.

Shop 174: Jingnan First Hot Pot (Jingnan Diyi Shuan).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k24l58.jpg)

This is a massive farmhouse-style restaurant. It must be the largest hot pot place in Beijing, and there is free parking in the courtyard.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/brcmfh.jpg)

The owner has their own farm, which provides their own vegetables and homemade tofu.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/i5bmia.jpg)

No matter how many people are in your group, you get a private room. These private spaces are great for chatting and are not as noisy as a main dining hall.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/l2qmd5.jpg)

Besides hot pot, they have a full range of stir-fried dishes and snacks.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/enjzhk.jpg)

The quality of the meat is good, and there is no foam in the broth after boiling the meat.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7umeli.jpg)

The red willow skewer barbecue (hongliu kaorou) is 15 yuan per skewer, which is a very fair price.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tahpwl.jpg)

Address: No. 1 Jia, Qingzheng Road, Xueying Village, Panggezhuang, Daxing District.

Shop 175: Runde Xuan Halal Restaurant.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cx4v12.jpg)

It follows the Daxing tradition of having spacious halal restaurants. You can see the huge sign from the Line 4 subway.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k81sr6.jpg)

This place is a full-service halal restaurant with over a hundred different dishes.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/35ic7h.jpg)

They serve hot pot, lamb spine (yangxiezi), and flour-based dishes like pot stickers (guotie).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nwq1qr.jpg)

The beef in the stewed beef brisket with potatoes is delicious.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2slxho.jpg)

Address: No. 14 Hongsheng Road, North Second Street, Xihongmen, Daxing District (300 meters north of Xihongmen Subway Station, on the east side of the road).

No. 176: Azhen Taste Rice Noodles (Azhen Weidao Mifan).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ts6i84.jpg)

This shop moved here from its original location in Weigongcun and occupies two floors. They offer two choices: chicken rice noodles and beef rice noodles.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/x2uq9d.jpg)

Rice noodles are common in many regions, but this place serves Xinjiang-style rice noodles.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3v7rtt.jpg)

I had the cold chicken rice noodles, mild spicy, but it was still a bit too spicy for me.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0fwd36.jpg)

Address: Building 5, 2nd Floor, No. 18 Minda West Road.

No. 177: HI HELLO Western-style Grilled Rice.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sz2lgj.jpg)

This is one of the few halal shops in Weigongcun that does not serve Xinjiang or Northwest Chinese cuisine; they make Western-style fast food.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/q0szcn.jpg)

It is very busy at lunchtime. You order and pay by phone yourself, which is very convenient.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/o24cqo.jpg)

I had the spaghetti with steak, and a large portion was only 38 yuan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/19a1qg.jpg)

Address: Shop 4, Ground Floor, Building 2, Weibohao Garden.

No. 178: Musefilin Xinjiang Restaurant.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/p773uq.jpg)

Although Xinjiang restaurants are common, this one is worth recommending first because the food tastes good, and second, the decor is quite modern, making it suitable for dates.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f7nkuf.jpg)

The guy at the front desk is a Uyghur, and he is very handsome.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fexfei.jpg)

The yogurt is very sweet and has a good texture.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4dojux.jpg)

The grilled lamb liver is truly fresh and tender; I wanted more after eating it.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3exejf.jpg)

The spicy lamb trotters (hula yangti) are mild spicy and very flavorful.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f2lsdz.jpg)

Thin-skinned steamed buns (baopi baozi) are a Xinjiang specialty. You have to wait a while for this dish because they wrap and steam them to order.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/29kcdc.jpg)

You have to pair grilled meat with flatbread (nang). Overall, the food at this shop is really good. Every dish is made with care, and it is worth a try.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/aosjwo.jpg)

Address: No. 27 Xidawang Road, Chaoyang District. It is right at the exit of Pingleyuan Station on Subway Line 14.

## China Mosque Travel Guide Linyi: Matou Mosque, Southern Shandong Hui Muslims and Ramadan Food
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: defdec867f2048e70ac882c9b27642193b9d51dfffa36de45ee8298b2858c953

# China Mosque Travel Guide Linyi: Matou Mosque, Southern Shandong Hui Muslims and Ramadan Food

Reposted from the web

Summary:
 This China mosque travel guide visits Matou Town in Tancheng County, Linyi, the Ming-era Matou Mosque built in 1405, the Hui Muslim community around it, mosque calligraphy halls, local halal food, Linyi grilled meat, pancakes with lamb chops, and Ramadan suhoor memories.

While driving south through Linyi City, Shandong Province this year, I learned about a large Ming Dynasty mosque in Matou Town, Tancheng County. I drove for nearly an hour from downtown Linyi to reach Matou Town.

Matou Town

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wt1g16.jpg)

Matou Town is an ancient Muslim town and a community where Hui Muslims live. The town is full of halal shops, and I even saw a Muslim martial arts school.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/uz2v9w.jpg)

The storefronts here still keep the traditional style of North China.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ucg0rs.jpg)

After walking through several small alleys, I found the Matou Mosque inside the village.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/x958dx.jpg)

Matou Mosque was first built during the Yongle period of the Ming Dynasty (1405). It covers 13 mu of land and is the largest mosque in northern Jiangsu and southern Shandong. It is well-preserved and is currently a cultural relic protection unit of Shandong Province.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1ygxhn.jpg)

In the courtyard just inside the gate, there are several stones gifted by elders from different places.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gexgdn.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mj097x.jpg)

There are several traditional calligraphy and painting exhibition halls in the courtyard. This is the first hall, and there is a second hall, both displaying non-religious artworks.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/j1fmo5.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yw8d9b.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/b6khh3.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/s28efk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cbdrq1.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/th8n4a.jpg)

Inside the mosque, there is a monument to an anti-Japanese hero, Zhang Hongyi, who was a Hui Muslim. Zhang Hongyi served as the political commissar of the Southern Shandong Independent Detachment and was known as the political commissar of the Railway Guerrillas. He died in 1945 while fighting against Japanese and puppet troops. The martyr Zhang Hongyi is buried in the Martyrs' Cemetery in Feixian County, Linyi City.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ao8oa2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/uf09n8.jpg)

There is also a women's prayer hall here.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/eav8ua.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/squgjz.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/i69fwd.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m8yj8x.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g3u5dc.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/e04amv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4xhi3c.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bcfh41.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tr3ral.jpg)

This ancient well was built in the third year of the Yongle period of the Ming Dynasty. In 1939, General Luo Ronghuan led the 115th Division of the Eighth Route Army into Tancheng. Eighth Route Army liaison officer Zhai Xinya, guided by a Hui youth named Lan Zhaofa, used the excuse of inviting an imam to slaughter a cow to enter the mosque's well pavilion. Under the cover of night, they took a shortcut to approach the enemy's blockhouse, destroyed the 'turtle shell' fortification, and successfully persuaded 360 puppet soldiers to surrender. Because of this, Lan Zhaofa honorably joined the Matou Hui Detachment.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7jaloh.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/atk15u.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y0vgkd.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/24ymrn.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ai4fxz.jpg)

There are several pieces of calligraphy on the walls of the corridor.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/b47tj5.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0ebxds.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/v63ent.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8ga9cj.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cp8noi.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tmwvp9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/18qcrt.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5kvrgd.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/geome8.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/szgtkd.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wuzutp.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pr3web.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5spu80.jpg)

There is a Hui Muslim culture exhibition hall in the courtyard, but it does not have any exhibits inside yet.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yf47mx.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/iqtuj0.jpg)

The list of donations (niyati) specifically marks our Han Chinese brothers and sisters.

Nanbeidao Mosque

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ghauh0.jpg)

Nanbeidao Mosque in downtown Linyi was first built during the Hongwu period of the Ming Dynasty. After being rebuilt many times, it is now a modern building. It has a floor area of 1,235 square meters and a total site area of 8,995 square meters. The area around the mosque is filled with halal beef and mutton shops.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lrq7v6.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/62uqvq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fvhyds.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vnendm.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wn2yds.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/88784w.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sip79o.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/em206o.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vgpsek.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/t78vfk.jpg)

Nanbeidao was formed by merging Nandao Village and Beidao Village, and this area is also a residential community for Hui Muslims.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/te8rxw.jpg)

Linyi Minzu Restaurant Main Branch

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7uc2bs.jpg)

Our friends in Linyi invited us to eat local Linyi specialties at the largest halal restaurant in the city. This is also where Hui Muslims hold weddings. I heard this is not even the largest location, as the flagship store of Minzu Restaurant is even bigger. The owner is a local Hui Muslim and a classmate of my friend.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ufwlm9.jpg)

Linyi University is also super huge. It is known as one of the largest universities in Asia, covering an area of over 6,000 mu. Linyi University once offered Arabic language courses, but they have now canceled all minor language courses, not just Arabic.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/c9urxq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/at18i0.jpg)

The people of Linyi seem to have an obsession with being big. The portions of food here are also huge. The four of us, plus a baby, ordered four dishes and only finished half of them.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1p323k.jpg)

Stir-fried chicken (chaoji) is a must-eat in Linyi. You can find stir-fried chicken shops everywhere, but most of them are not halal.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/o9hzgq.jpg)

Fahim especially likes eating Linyi pancakes (jianbing). We ate every bit of this plate of pancakes rolled with lamb chops.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jzzio5.jpg)

I originally wanted to drink some meat porridge (sa) in the morning, but during Ramadan, the shops were not open yet after we finished our pre-dawn meal (suhoor). It is best to drink the first pot of meat porridge around 5 a.m.
