## Halal Food Guide Tianjin: Japanese Restaurants, Western Dining and Hui Muslim Local Food
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 09ce39b1fde60a0b046a876436fc387ae1416308bd38ecd3b9d7a7d9709fe17c

# Halal Food Guide Tianjin: Japanese Restaurants, Western Dining and Hui Muslim Local Food

Reposted from the web

Summary: This Tianjin halal food guide covers Muslim-friendly Japanese restaurants, Western dining, no-alcohol halal options, Hui Muslim local food, Tianmu village, and practical notes for halal travelers.

Tianjin Halal Food Map is presented here as a firsthand travel account in clear English, beginning with this scene: What attracts me most to Tianjin is the large number of halal Western and Japanese restaurants. Excluding noodle shops, Tianjin has more halal restaurants that do not sell alcohol than Beijing. The account keeps its focus on Halal Travel, Yiwu Food, Beijing while preserving the names, places, food, and historical details from the Chinese source.

What attracts me most to Tianjin is the large number of halal Western and Japanese restaurants. Excluding noodle shops, Tianjin has more halal restaurants that do not sell alcohol than Beijing.

Because of its history as a foreign concession, Tianjin has many Western and Japanese restaurants. Since there is a lot of civilian exchange between Tianjin and Japan, the quality of the Japanese food here is reliable.

Niaohe Kappo Cuisine

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/n7smtd.jpg)

You need to book in advance to eat at this Japanese yakitori restaurant, which has a high rating on Dianping. It is located at 125 Hami Road. We did not check beforehand and arrived to find all the seats were booked, so we could not eat there. We just took some photos of the interior and will try again next time.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cmx9uk.jpg)

The wooden partitions inside and the wall decorations have a strong Japanese style.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4217xm.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/67iz1k.jpg)

Qianmu Halal Japanese Restaurant

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wuo3de.jpg)

Qianmu has two locations in Tianjin. One is in Guoyuan, not far from Tianmu Village, and the other is in Shuangjie. We visited the Guoyuan branch so we could also explore Tianmu, which is a village for Hui Muslims in the suburbs of Tianjin, quite far from the city center.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mym38k.jpg)

This restaurant is also busy, but to be safe, I called ahead to book a table.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5xga07.jpg)

Although marble soda (ramune) is very popular in Japan and has a long history, it was first invented in the UK. The Japanese name for it, ramune, is a transliteration of the English word lemonade.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nyh41q.jpg)

Marble soda (ramune)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xli1o1.jpg)

Battleship sushi platter (gunkan maki)

The battleship sushi platter is perfect for someone like me who wants to try many different flavors. You get one piece of each, and then you can just order more of the ones you like.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/siy6de.jpg)

Natto

Natto is a common Japanese fermented soybean dish. People usually mix it with rice. It is sticky and stringy. Some say it smells bad, but I do not think so. I can swallow it, but I do not think it tastes good.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qokkbe.jpg)

Grilled platter

This is a Japanese-style grilled combo. It has chicken skewers and grilled okra. I think the grilled fish balls are the best.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/opbqbw.jpg)

Baked cheese fish cake (chikuwa)

Chikuwa is a type of Japanese fish cake. The middle looks like it was grilled, and the ends are yellow and white. The texture is similar to a fish ball.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qfeo9s.jpg)

Salt-grilled ginkgo nuts

This is my first time eating salt-grilled ginkgo nuts. The shells are already cracked. The nut inside is tender and slightly bitter.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0vo82a.jpg)

Eight-piece sashimi platter

The eight-piece sashimi platter costs 288 yuan. Each type of sashimi has five slices. The fish is fresh and does not taste fishy. A friend from Tianjin once told me a trick to identify fresh seafood: if it does not taste fishy when you eat it, the ingredients are fresh.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/43iiws.jpg)

Live eel rice

The live eel is definitely fresh, which makes the rice taste savory and delicious. Japanese food is never cheap, and this meal cost about 150 yuan per person.

Luoyan Halal Western Restaurant

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/978u1f.jpg)

Luoyan is named after the owner's child. This is a smoke-free and alcohol-free Western restaurant, and it is closed on Mondays.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nper8b.jpg)

The interior of the small Western-style building caught my eye as soon as I walked in. The restaurant has two floors, and we chose to sit on the second floor to look down at the lobby.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hy2sip.jpg)

Beijing really does not have this kind of standard European-style halal Western food. Halal Western food in Beijing is usually mixed with Middle Eastern cuisine.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zpp9b8.jpg)

European-style Western food usually includes alcohol, but this place offers non-alcoholic champagne and red wine, so the Western dining atmosphere is just as good.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/p8muyl.jpg)

This bottle of imported French halal champagne costs 288 yuan at the restaurant. We found the same one on Taobao for 94 yuan, so interested friends can go try it. The halal champagne tastes sour and astringent, and I am not sure if it is authentic. Because of this bottle of wine, our meal cost more than 200 yuan per person.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7y4i0p.jpg)

Here is a little bit of Islamic legal knowledge: although most regions accept non-alcoholic wine as a halal drink, a small number of jurists believe that non-alcoholic wine should not be consumed either, because it carries the suspicion of intending to drink alcohol. Even if you hold up a glass of water and say 'let us toast with water instead of wine,' that glass of water might also become impermissible to drink. Although we believe that the prohibition of alcohol is because it causes intoxication, some jurists only follow the literal meaning without considering the underlying intent.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/msp5is.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/03i3ep.jpg)

Wasabi Avocado Shrimp Salad

The large shrimp are fresh and big, and the slight heat from the wasabi makes it perfect for people on a diet.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dsr5f8.jpg)

Malaysian durian pizza

The durian pizza is very fragrant and has plenty of durian filling. I will mention another Western restaurant's durian pizza later, which has even more filling.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/o9rorj.jpg)

Burmese giant tiger prawn with pan-seared foie gras

It is rare to find halal foie gras, so we splurged a little this time. The four of us each had one bite of the prawn and foie gras, and it was all gone.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/z2dk7n.jpg)

Russian-style pot-stewed beef with garlic baguette

Pot-stewed beef is a classic Russian dish. The beef is stewed with tomatoes inside the pot and covered with a layer of puff pastry. The pastry is very crispy. After one bite, I knew the upcoming Beef Wellington would be great.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7176t3.jpg)

Beef Wellington

The signature of Beef Wellington is the puff pastry wrapping. Most Western restaurants use shortening for the pastry, which often contains lard and is not halal. Maiweitang in Beijing used to serve Beef Wellington, but the Hui Muslim owner stopped serving it, so we cannot eat it there anymore.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jpw5ci.jpg)

Thick-cut filet mignon

Filet mignon is beef tenderloin steak. It has a chewier texture than Beef Wellington. After ordering, the waiter will ask how you want it cooked, ranging from 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, to well-done. Well-done steak can be dry, so I suggest 5 to 7 minutes. Ours is cooked to 7 minutes.

Totoro Canteen (Longmao Shitang)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/85o5x8.jpg)

If you think the Japanese food I recommended earlier is too expensive, you can try Totoro Canteen. The average cost per person is under 100 yuan. It is located opposite the China Huaxia Bank in Hengxing World.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/s1b5jp.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dm6sxz.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bxoovb.jpg)

Non-alcoholic mojito

Their mojito tastes better than just okay.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qkqmp3.jpg)

Wasabi octopus

Wasabi octopus in small shops is usually canned, so it is fine for a quick taste.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/w0ad67.jpg)

Salmon sashimi

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bjrz8a.jpg)

Queen eel rice

The eel rice is topped with bonito flakes (katsuobushi), which are thin shavings made from skipjack tuna and a common ingredient in Japanese cuisine.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hiyref.jpg)

Seafood ramen

A conservative estimate is that there are about ten halal Japanese restaurants currently in Tianjin, and I have saved a few more to visit.

Yiweizhai Halal rice noodle rolls (changfen)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yglz9r.jpg)

I visited Yeji Rice Noodle Rolls years ago, and later learned they opened this shop, which has now been open for five years. The menu has almost all the Cantonese snacks, and it is also a restaurant that does not sell alcohol.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qtzweu.jpg)

This place serves bigger portions than Yeji. I tried the most iconic version of every Cantonese snack, and they were all delicious. I wish they would open a branch in Beijing, as all the halal Cantonese restaurants there have closed down.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cqhq8t.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vnt0cn.jpg)

Seafood congee

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mg6swl.jpg)

Hong Kong-style shrimp dumpling noodles

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/81s8g1.jpg)

Two-flavor steamed rice rolls (changfen)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1tyop1.jpg)

Stir-fried rice noodles with beef (ganchao niuhe).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/aasrgp.jpg)

Custard bun (naihuangbao)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wmvaci.jpg)

Cured beef claypot rice (baozai fan)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9ki9gd.jpg)

While we ate, we saw the owner deveining shrimp. Each of their shrimp dumplings contains two large shrimp, which is very generous.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2kuq9k.jpg)

Jin Jin Halal Curry Restaurant

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jjsy63.jpg)

This is a halal Western restaurant near Nankai Joy City. The address is in the picture, and it is also an alcohol-free restaurant.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7vdqkr.jpg)

The restaurant is a loft inside an apartment building. The owner is the only person working there, and she is also the chef. She is from Tianjin and very talkative. When she saw us, she greeted us with salaam first.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lvagdp.jpg)

The two most popular dishes are the Beef Wellington and the Musang King durian pizza. The pizza has the most toppings I have ever seen, with a thick layer of durian.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xgxqai.jpg)

Musang King durian pizza

I really liked the taste of the roast chicken with lemon. Squeeze the juice from a lemon slice onto the chicken pieces; the crispy chicken pairs perfectly with the refreshing lemon.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/t9mo2b.jpg)

French-style lemon country roast chicken

The beef Wellington did not impress me. The steak quality is not as good as at Luoyan, but their dishes are affordable, costing about 100 yuan per person.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/43niz4.jpg)

Beef Wellington

For drinks, I chose a non-alcoholic mojito and an Oreo yogurt potted plant. The mojito tasted just okay, but the portion of the yogurt potted plant was huge.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/b51owg.jpg)

Non-alcoholic mojito

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cd78gx.jpg)

Oreo yogurt potted plant

That is all for the restaurant introduction for now. If you are interested in Tianjin snacks but do not know what to pick, I suggest visiting the Northwest Corner. Walk around the street in front of the Great Southern Mosque (Qingzhen Nandasi). Although it is not as lively as it used to be after the renovations and many traditional snacks are gone, there are still plenty of options.

Fried bean sprout roll (juanquan)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bdggod.jpg)

A juanquan is a wrap with fillings you can choose yourself. The deep-fried roll is filled with bean sprouts. You can only find this in Tianjin, not in Beijing.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/18r1j5.jpg)

Huiji Tea Soup (Huiji Chatang)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4q70sl.jpg)

This is a thick dessert, but it is not as sweet as you might think. It has a light, refreshing taste and is quite good when eaten hot.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/68jof0.jpg)

Wenji Rice Cake (gaogan)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m8pb0v.jpg)

The outer layer is made of rice and filled with various flavors. They cost 2 yuan each and are also sweet. These three snacks are quite good, and you should eat them while they are hot. You can come and try them during the Spring Festival holiday.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/356d39.jpg)

Halal Zhangji

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/adiwdc.jpg)

Zhangji sells fried skewers in the Northwest Corner. Interestingly, they also added steamed rice rolls (changfen). If you want to try all kinds of snacks in the Northwest Corner, you can try their steamed rice rolls. The taste is also quite good.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9zfnw7.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/th9lr2.jpg)

Besides eating and walking around in Tianjin, you can also listen to crosstalk at the Tianjin Deyunshe. The venue is much larger than the one in Beijing. You need to book tickets on the Damai app. During the Spring Festival, Deyunshe has closed for the season, and you will have to wait until after the holiday for performances to resume.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vnkcce.jpg)

Deyunshe is very popular now, and theater tickets are hard to get. We booked front-row seats a week in advance for 240 yuan per person.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/phzmda.jpg)

Listening to crosstalk live is all about feeling the happy atmosphere. It is very relaxing to enjoy the show while drinking tea.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3qv094.jpg)

However, famous performers rarely appear in small theaters. The ones holding up the show are all Guo Degang's apprentices, and I do not recognize any of them.

X-RAY Art Lab

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5ztebs.jpg)

This is a DIY embroidery shop opened by a friend in Tianjin. I customized a tag and a handbag here for my son, Fahim.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6aw1mx.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pl14mo.jpg)

I made a hanging ornament for Fahim embroidered with the words "Tawhid" (the Oneness of Allah) to help him remember the most important thing in life.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4d1fj8.jpg)

The English spelling of Faheem is Faheem.

Here is a quick tip on Islamic law: cartoon characters are halal for children. According to The Lawful and the Prohibited in Islam by Yusuf al-Qaradawi, photos are allowed. Statues that cast a shadow are forbidden, but children's toys are an exception because kids need them and there is no intent of idol worship.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zshpni.jpg)

I have often seen elderly people at the mosque criticize teenagers for wearing clothes with images on them. They really hate it. It is already rare for these young people to come to the mosque, and some are now afraid to enter because they worry about being scolded by the elders for an unintentional mistake. Besides, your criticism has no basis.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7245gn.jpg)

Search for "x-ray art lab" on Taobao or add the owner on WeChat at w287302278 to place an order.

## Muslim Travel Guide Beijing Changying: Hui Township Market, Halal Food and Local Community
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 1712636a0507cbf6375aea384c87c5d8c021184d575d215a61b03a0a9ee62e2d

# Muslim Travel Guide Beijing Changying: Hui Township Market, Halal Food and Local Community

Reposted from the web

Summary:
 Changying Hui Township in Beijing has a lively weekend market with halal food, local snacks, and vendors from the Hui Muslim community. This account keeps the market route, dishes, prices where given, and photographs in the original order.

I went to the big market in Changying on Sunday morning. I worked near Changying for nine years and only changed jobs last year, and then the big market opened there. The market is open every day until noon, and it is busiest on weekends.

In the morning, I first had breakfast at Qingyanfang on Changying Middle Road, where I ordered steamed buns (xiaolongbao), lucky bags (fudai), pumpkin porridge, and purple rice porridge. Lucky bags are similar to egg fritters (jidan guozi), but they are rarely seen in Beijing. There were no halal steamed buns before, but some shops have started making them in recent years.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/03om7k.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7shq8r.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f3f12o.jpg)

After breakfast, I walked north along the Changying intersection to reach the big market at the southeast corner of Changying Sports Park. There are many halal shops at the market.

We first bought beef head meat at Chen's to take home for a stir-fry. Their shop's water kettle sign (tangping pai) was made by my friend Dong Yidong, and this traditional style of sign is very rare now.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jnwrr2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qlhhdx.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wc6pzi.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/z3ecve.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ykyx21.jpg)

The market also has crispy fried dough snacks (gezhihe) from Hui Muslims in Tongzhou, and they also sell fermented mung bean milk (douzhier) from Baoji; many people buy it, but it was sold out when we got there.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9wayk5.jpg)

There is a Huadu chicken stall at the market, and we bought a free-range chicken to take home for a big plate chicken (dapanji) dish. Huadu has a halal chicken slaughter and processing plant in Luanping, Hebei, which supplies the Beijing market and also exports to Japan. They have opened online shops in recent years, and much of the halal chicken you can buy online now comes from them.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ywf0sm.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/q9irt2.jpg)

The market also has all kinds of beef, mutton, fruits, and vegetables, especially free samples; we tried muskmelon, nectarines, apples, and watermelon, which made my son very happy.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zrcybq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/d1g287.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wno0cx.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tjbnwk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bu5td7.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gwi03z.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9lm5g1.jpg)

The northernmost row of the market is the snack area, where a noodle shop sells cold noodles, which are perfect for summer.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gg51wp.jpg)

The two most popular snacks at the market are crispy fried cakes (cuipi zhagao) and freshly baked naan bread (kaonang), both of which have long lines. The naan shop uses a bamboo charcoal oven, and the naan they make is especially fragrant with a texture different from electric-baked ones. We arrived a bit late and missed the freshly baked meat pies (kaobaozi), but if you go earlier, they are surely very fragrant when they come out of the oven.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qlt46g.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wr94bv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gztkqq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/32f8pv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/w49p64.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fc9k1g.jpg)

After visiting the Changying market, we took the bus for three stops to Guanzhuang Road to eat at Bu Hui Qiqihar Barbecue. I haven't been here for several years, and it is very popular on weekend afternoons now; we waited for half an hour to get a table. They now offer a nine-grid set meal with meat and shrimp, which is quite rich, and the set also includes almond tofu, sliced frozen pears, and small sticky bean buns (niandoubao). It was my first time eating Northeast-style sticky bean buns, which are made of yellow rice flour wrapped around a kidney bean filling; they aren't sweet, with a light flavor of yellow rice and kidney beans that everyone liked. For our main course, we ordered stone pot bibimbap, basin bibimbap, and cold noodles. My mother-in-law tried stone pot rice (bibimbap) for the first time and really liked the taste. The side dishes inside were also very nutritious. Cold noodles (lengmian) are the main reason I come to this restaurant. The weather is getting hotter now, and a bowl of these sweet and sour noodles is especially refreshing.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/10juhu.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/amqszn.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y9druh.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a42ir9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qdcsj6.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/e4cpio.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/q885oj.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5v3uxi.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3kyzky.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fx9b8p.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9jkmqp.jpg)

---

## Ramadan in China 2026: Qur'an, Hadith, Tarawih and Muslim Strength for Fasting
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 4f5cebfa0d3846b89d2bfeef55ed69ada08f67eafa296ad780f7ebc2c8eebc04

# Ramadan in China 2026: Qur'an, Hadith, Tarawih and Muslim Strength for Fasting

Reposted from the web

Summary:
 This Ramadan essay reminds Muslims of the value of fasting, the reward of Ramadan, the hadith about Paradise, Hell, and the chained devils, and the author's own effort to fast and pray Tarawih despite health fears.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/651ycr.jpg)

"O you who believe, fasting has been prescribed for you as it was prescribed for those before you, so that you may become mindful of Allah" (2:183), Allah has clarified in the Qur'an that fasting is not only the command of the Prophet's era, it is also the command of the saints of the past, and it is also the command of us and our descendants after the Prophet. Fasting is a behavior that can be seen in almost all mainstream religious groups that are currently accessible, whether it is Islam, Buddhism, Taoism, Christianity, Catholicism... Fasting is almost in a parallel relationship with sacrifice. Whenever people fast, they always offer sacrifices devoutly and interact with others gently.

Many people, whenever someone says to them: "Ramadan is here, you should fast", they will always find all kinds of weird reasons to excuse themselves, such as: I have a cold, I have to work in the fields, it is inconvenient for me to go to work, I... Anyway, there are a lot of various reasons, in short, there is one purpose: they do not want to fast! As everyone knows, Allah said in the Qur'an, "Fasting is better for you, if you only knew" (2:184). The benefits of fasting have been repeated over and over again by scholars and imams throughout the ages. I will not go into details because anyone who tries to escape the fast will find excuses anyway, and anyone who wants to complete the fast will always overcome all kinds of difficulties to complete his fast.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/t94jcy.jpg)

In the blink of an eye, this year's Ramadan has passed for twenty-eight or nine days. In just one or two days, Ramadan will wave to us and say "goodbye". If you want to observe such a noble fast again, you can only wait until next year. Maybe many people will think that if you just wait another year, there is nothing worth mentioning. In fact, what I want to say is: In fact, for many people, in many cases, this year may be their last Ramadan, and missing this Ramadan may be their biggest regret in life. Natural disasters, man-made disasters, and diseases warn us all the time to cherish our time and our immediate health. However, many people are unaware of it and always feel that time and health are the cheapest and longest-lasting, so we procrastinate again and again when completing the various missions set by Allah for us.

The Messenger of Allah said: “When Ramadan comes, all the gates of Paradise are opened, all the gates of Hell are closed, and all the demons are locked up (in chains). "[the two Sahih collections] This hadith tells us the nobility of Ramadan. Ramadan is a day of peace, tranquility, mercy, and a day when all demons are sent to "prisons" and imprisoned. In fact, what I want to say more about this hadith is: Since the devils are locked up during Ramadan, the doors of hell are closed. Only when the door of heaven is open and the gods come down to earth to wish us peace, if we commit a sin due to our own reasons, what excuse do we have to excuse ourselves? Normally we would say that we commit sins because we are tempted by the devil, but during Ramadan the devil will definitely not be blamed for the sins we commit.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2r213y.jpg)

The nobility of Ramadan is that to the gates of Hell being closed and the demons being shackled and locked up, any good deeds done during Ramadan will be rewarded twice as much as on other days. What is even more valuable are those who fast in "Ramadan", and Allah personally rewards those who fast [the two Sahih collections]. Another hadith promise is even more exciting: "As long as a person fasts for one day on the road of Allah, Allah will keep him away from the Fire for seventy years because of this day of fasting." [the two Sahih collections] Many people may think that the "seventy years" here is a unit of time. In fact, the "seventy years" here is a unit of space (distance), just like the astronomical word "light year" we often hear. The so-called "light year" is the distance traveled by light in one year, and we all know that the speed of light is 300,000 kilometers per second. The sun is about 150 million kilometers away from the earth. It takes about 8.3 minutes for the light from the sun to reach the earth. If the "seventy years" here are "seventy light years", we can't imagine how far this distance is. But even if it is not "light years", even if we walk on our feet, seventy years is still a very long distance. Since the rewards of fasting are so great, how can we be willing to give up?

I thought I would bid farewell to Ramadan completely from last year, but Allah, I tried to fast this year without any serious physical discomfort. Then I fasted day after day, and then prayed "Tarawih" day after day. When we are devout to the intention for Allah and work hard for it, then Allah will not let the effort of His servants go to waste.

Ultimate question: Are you fasting this year?

---

## Best Halal Food Beijing 2025: JM Cafe, Ningxia Hot Pot, Xinjiang BBQ and Hui Muslim Snacks
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 27f22ab0a70ebffbf1724209dea878e23f0ad096416782968d244fcbac6509a3

# Best Halal Food Beijing 2025: JM Cafe, Ningxia Hot Pot, Xinjiang BBQ and Hui Muslim Snacks

Reposted from the web

Summary:
 This Beijing halal food map issue 52 introduces an AI restaurant-search tool and then covers JM CAFE & BAKERY, Zanjin Ningxia spicy hot pot, Xinjiang Aimaier Barbecue, Samarkand, Tianjin yellow-broth ramen, Lianying shaomai, Jingzhenxuan, and more local halal restaurants.

My official account now has an AI chatbot. You can ask questions in the chat, and the AI will help you find restaurant information to make searching easier.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/x6pfox.jpg)

It is still in the testing phase, so the answers might not be perfect. Even so, it is much better than the old keyword replies. Otherwise, I would have to set up countless keywords to handle all kinds of unusual questions.

The halal restaurants featured in this issue are as follows:

1. JM CAFE & BAKERY

2. Zanjin Ningxia Spicy Hot Pot (lahuhu)

3. Xinjiang Aimaier Barbecue

4. Samarkand (Hamuerhan) Workers' Stadium Branch

5. Tianjin Fengwei Yellow Broth Ramen (huangtang lamian)

6. Roma Restaurant Bar

7. Lianying Steamed Dumplings (shaomai) · Grassland Red Pomegranate

8. Zhizi Barbecue Zhang

9. Mufu Halal Spicy Hot Pot · Beef Noodles

10. Highland yak meat revolving mini hot pot

11. FIRENZE Italian Restaurant

12. Jingzhenxuan

1. JM CAFE & BAKERY

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qonxl8.jpg)

JM is a chain brand founded by a Hui Muslim. JM are his initials, and the owner is a big fan of Jay Chou, so the shop always plays his songs.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jdns6z.jpg)

This is the White Pagoda Mosque branch. The cafe and bakery are close to each other. The owner says he insists on using halal ingredients, does not sell alcohol, and all drinks in the shop are non-alcoholic.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9teedj.jpg)

Their bread and desserts taste great. I tried everything for you. The business is booming now, and it is a popular spot on social media.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/grp996.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1czwbq.jpg)

The cafe has a terrace on the second floor where you can take photos with the White Pagoda in the background.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ftdl3p.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/q7ccze.jpg)

Everything is a non-alcoholic drink.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/eezxot.jpg)

They do not have a halal sign hanging outside, but the owner promises that all ingredients in his shops are halal and insists on not selling alcohol. I think this approach might be more reliable than just hanging a halal sign.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fc3s3y.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nylsaq.jpg)

The owner of JM says they will soon open new branches on Niujie and Daji Lane. They are expected to open in August, and the Daji Lane shop will be quite large. I am really looking forward to it.

2. Zanjin Ningxia Spicy Hot Pot (lahuhu)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f83vlg.jpg)

This is a Ningxia-style spicy hot pot (malatang) shop. Its specialties are Ningxia cold skin noodles (liangpi), spicy paste (lahuhu), and stir-fried rice with fermented chili (zaolajiao).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4vj9ph.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lwkug2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/39ytjb.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yd0r2s.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/o80p3i.jpg)

Ningxia-style spicy hot pot does not use sesame paste. It uses chili oil, which is very similar to Sichuan-style skewers (chuanchuan).

3. Xinjiang Aimaier Barbecue

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ivhbf2.jpg)

This is a unique Xinjiang barbecue shop. You pick your meat skewers from a freezer when you walk in, and then they grill them for you.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8isbf2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dzt3k9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0u0i19.jpg)

Eating barbecue here is quite satisfying because they use charcoal-grilled skewers. It is a chain store, and they have another location at Dawanglu.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/evf5im.jpg)

4. Samarkand (Hamuerhan) Workers' Stadium Branch

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/v2to4t.jpg)

If you want to see the best-decorated halal restaurant in Beijing, just come to the Samarkand (Hamuerhan) Gongti branch.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/iizyc4.jpg)

Samarkand is a high-end brand under the Western Mahua group. They first opened at the Fengtai headquarters base, which also has a lot of style, but none are as luxurious as this Gongti branch.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8ji20b.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hfm9fa.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bol02a.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1q6ji8.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tjnm63.jpg)

The highlight of the Gongti branch is the afternoon tea. I chose the one called Chief's Afternoon Tea, which is enough for 4 to 6 people to eat, drink, and chat for the whole afternoon.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5xh1ui.jpg)

The set includes sand-boiled coffee, black tea, desserts, and fruit.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1mzmcv.jpg)

They also have yogurt with milk skin (naipizi suannai), which is now a standard item in Northwest restaurants.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/u15oa9.jpg)

Overall, Samarkand focuses more on style than substance, and the taste of the dishes needs improvement. However, I still really like the series of fast food restaurants from Western Mahua.

5. Tianjin Fengwei Yellow Broth Ramen (huangtang lamian)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/by6azz.jpg)

Yellow broth pulled noodles (huangtang lamian) are a specialty of Tianjin, and now there is a halal version in Beijing.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ai90n5.jpg)

This shop is run by people from Tianjin and Qinghai. The Tianjin staff handle the seasoning, while the Qinghai staff handle the pulled noodles and barbecue, keeping the flavor true to Tianjin style.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gpkdk2.jpg)

The reason the broth for these pulled noodles is yellow is because curry is added to it.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qrm9zw.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yfhigk.jpg)

The sauce brushed on the barbecue is on the sweet side.

6. Roma Restaurant Bar

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/utdgbj.jpg)

This Roma Bistro is run by a Pakistani friend. Although the place is small, the menu features a mix of dishes from Africa, Mexico, Russia, Turkey, India, Pakistan, and Italy. It is surprisingly delicious and cheap.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ocpxv4.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3nxz59.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xnbsy9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ue3om0.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yioyk3.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/s8stov.jpg)

Their set meals are a great deal, costing only 50 to 60 yuan per person. The Pakistani kitchen staff are very particular about their cooking; the biryani is excellent, and the drinks are well-made.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/179cy5.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4bw30x.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4j6w0f.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4kvh0x.jpg)

7. Lianying Steamed Dumplings (shaomai) · Grassland Red Pomegranate

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1tzx6k.jpg)

Lianying Shaomai, a famous restaurant from Ulanqab, has opened a flagship store in Qianmen that specializes in halal Mongolian food.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2icl9l.jpg)

I highly recommend their Inner Mongolian pot tea (guochai), which is brewed fresh to order and contains milk tofu and beef jerky.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k8tz5o.jpg)

The Mongolian meat sausage and hand-grabbed meat (shouba rou) are also signature dishes, and all the ingredients come from Inner Mongolia.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a67dr6.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/soxciy.jpg)

Steamed dumplings (shaomai) are their signature dish. You can watch them make them fresh in the open kitchen, and my favorite is the lamb leg steamed dumplings (shaomai).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hfuwqh.jpg)

The most expensive dish is the whole roasted lamb for 2,000 yuan, which is actually a fair price. The meat is very tender and the skin is crispy.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/07lds6.jpg)

You can also watch Mongolian dancing while you eat.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/07k6cb.jpg)

The stir-fried wild onion with lamb (shacong chaoyangrou) is also delicious.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0qzaq4.jpg)

The Inner Mongolian style sweet and sour beef (guobaorou) is made with strips of beef.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hmexp7.jpg)

The lamb spine hot pot (yangxiezi guo) has a light flavor that is just right. You can add more meat after finishing the spine, making it a great choice for seniors and children.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/v0y0vx.jpg)

The second floor has private rooms and a terrace for parties. It is very spacious and has an underground parking lot, with an average cost of under 100 yuan per person.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3m4p0f.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/z9m0bk.jpg)

8. Zhizi Barbecue Zhang

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ryu0jq.jpg)

The tomato hot pot restaurant at Caishikou has turned into a halal iron plate barbecue (zhizi kaorou) shop, but they still serve the tomato hot pot.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4jj88j.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/13a28y.jpg)

I saw paddlefish (yazuiyu) on the menu and decided to try it since I had never eaten it before.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/l8adts.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dhtc9h.jpg)

The paddlefish (yazuiyu) meat is tender and has no small bones, so it is good for kids. The meat is marinated beforehand.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/as1ve2.jpg)

The rattan pepper beef (tengjiao niurou) comes from Xingji at Niujie.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5ox1wh.jpg)

Someone helps you grill the whole time, the service is pretty good, and it costs about 100 per person.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jbrl7n.jpg)

9. Mufu Halal Spicy Hot Pot · Beef Noodles

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nyld19.jpg)

A newly opened halal spicy hot pot (malatang) in Wangfujing is owned by the same person as the beef noodle shop next door, who is from Linxia.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/56rgnh.jpg)

The owner studied Islamic scriptures in the past and has been running this shop in Wangfujing for three years.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mnxphd.jpg)

Their spicy hot pot has many options. You can choose the dry-mixed Tianshui style or the Sichuan style. You can also pick the spice level. The mild version uses Gansu chili peppers, which are fragrant but not too spicy, and you can choose even spicier levels.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3st8sw.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4uwerv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9jmoa1.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/if4zj2.jpg)

Their grilled skewers are especially delicious because the ingredients are high quality. The seasoning added to the spicy hot pot tastes just like Chongqing small noodles (xiaomian), which I really like.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a3jklq.jpg)

10. Highland yak meat revolving mini hot pot

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ttk6v3.png)

Alihan Grilled Meat and Mixed Noodles in Jinsong has been open for thirty years and is always busy. They recently added a yak meat conveyor belt hot pot.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bw4bkp.jpg)

There are many types of dishes and the prices are not expensive; you can eat for a few dozen yuan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/c277tt.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/btk1ab.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ojshsq.jpg)

They even have two flavors of kvass (kawas) to choose from.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4qhmi8.jpg)

What attracts me most is the yak beef rolls. They taste great in the hot pot, have a good chew, and are better than the meat at typical conveyor belt hot pot places.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cvimlm.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/n6b7ys.jpg)

Their mixed noodles (banmian) and barbecue are also signature dishes, and you can choose either.

11. FIRENZE Italian Restaurant

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/i6hmzz.jpg)

This is the first halal Italian-themed restaurant in the capital. It used to be an Italian restaurant that struggled, but now Pakistani owners have taken over. They kept the Italian flavors and added Indian and Pakistani dishes.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/l6h331.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ew9diq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4k2iwf.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pkgadn.jpg)

The lamb chops and pasta are very Italian. It is rare to find such authentic halal Italian food in Beijing.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wu88yo.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7jrkcc.jpg)

Seeing the crispy balls (pani puri) served with this dish reveals the chef's background.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pdh5f1.jpg)

The shop serves a special orange Americano. They stick to the Pakistani restaurant tradition of serving no alcohol. In the summer, you can sit in the small garden courtyard and eat barbecue. The average cost is over 100 yuan.

12. Jingzhenxuan

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bsysvb.jpg)

I spent Eid al-Adha (Qurban Festival) in Doudian. In the morning, I went to Jingzhenxuan and had their freshly made meat porridge and fried dough (youxiang).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/opi7uq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pjbns0.jpg)

Jingzhenxuan is located inside the Doudian Mosque, and they are very particular about their ingredients.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/iduf6z.jpg)

The owner gave Fahim a bottle of homemade sour plum drink (suanmeitang). It had a rich flavor and was great for cooling off.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gd18hr.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3bgeyq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1dwwqh.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vtiosu.jpg)

After the Friday prayer (Jumu'ah) at noon, we came back to Jingzhenxuan to eat their traditional halal dish, braised lamb strips (pa yangrou tiao).

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qwtec4.jpg)

Their stir-fried dishes are authentic, generous, and clean. If you have a large group, you can come here for the beef head feast, which requires at least 10 people to finish.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zo82ix.jpg)

---

## Muslim Travel Guide Toronto: Chinese Hui Muslim Eid al-Adha, Halal Noodles and Scarborough Mosque
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: 1b022f2577128761e5e57d45f9832aec0c6883b1f7b97aada823171da605f139

# Muslim Travel Guide Toronto: Chinese Hui Muslim Eid al-Adha, Halal Noodles and Scarborough Mosque

Reposted from the web

Summary: This Muslim travel guide to Toronto follows Eid al-Adha with the Chinese Muslim Association of Canada in Scarborough, halal Hui Muslim food, Fang Zhongshan-style spicy soup, halal noodles, Canadian diversity, Muslim communities, and reflections on immigrant life.

Celebrating Eid Al-Adha in the Hui Muslim Neighborhood of Toronto, Canada is presented here as a firsthand travel account in clear English, beginning with this scene: I traveled around Canada while attending the MDRT global life insurance conference this year. The account keeps its focus on Sanya Travel, Eid al-Adha, Muslim Travel while preserving the names, places, food, and historical details from the Chinese source.

I traveled around Canada while attending the MDRT global life insurance conference this year. I visited Toronto, Ottawa, Montreal, Calgary, and Vancouver, covering the most important cities from the east to the west coast. Since Eid al-Adha was approaching, I learned that the Chinese Muslim Association of Canada is in Toronto, so I made a special trip there to attend the holiday prayers.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hyqswg.jpg)

Toronto is Canada's largest city. It has a large population, sits near New York State in the U.S., and has a strong economy and convenient transportation. Many Chinese immigrants live here. The photos above and below show Lanzhou hand-pulled noodle shops (Lanzhou lamian) I spotted on the street, but neither of these two shops is halal.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jko191.jpg)

Don't worry, I will introduce a halal noodle shop later.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/e9sg11.jpg)

India is not currently issuing visas to Chinese citizens, so if you want to see Indian people, come to Canada. India is the top source of immigrants to Canada, and you can find Indian people everywhere here. They are clean and hygienic. The ones wearing turbans are Sikhs. There are over 700,000 Sikhs in Canada. They are often mistaken for Muslims, but Sikhs and Muslims are actually rivals. Sikh teachings seem designed to oppose Islam. For example, Sikhs do not eat any religious food, including halal food. If they don't know the source of the meat, they cannot eat it. They only eat meat slaughtered by their own people, which leads some to mistakenly think Sikhs are vegetarians. They are also not allowed to marry Muslims. Because Sikhs are a minority in India and face oppression, a large number of them have immigrated to North America as refugees.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k6rbl5.jpg)

The Chinese Muslim Association of Canada is located on the third floor of the Oriental Centre in Scarborough. It used to be on the second floor. In June 2024, the association president, Bao Lin Zhu, paid in full to purchase a property on the third floor for the association's use. The original second-floor location remains open as the Hui Muslim Food Culture Center.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ijyxrf.jpg)

The Fang Zhongshan spicy soup (hulatang) on the second floor is run by the president, who is from Henan province and a fellow townsman of Fang Zhongshan. The shop is authorized by Fang Zhongshan, but the taste of the spicy soup is completely different from the one in the Central Plains. It leans more toward the Xiaoyao Town style and is not as spicy.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5797nh.jpg)

The person on the far right is President Bao Lin. The chef on the left is also from Henan and immigrated to Canada to follow his children's studies.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/64t2uw.jpg)

Besides spicy soup and pan-fried buns (shuijianbao), the shop also prepares traditional Chinese Hui Muslim foods like fried dough (youxiang) for Eid al-Adha.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lv2uia.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/l2rleu.jpg)

The room next to the spicy soup shop was the association's original prayer room. Now that they have the third-floor property, this space is used for reception, office work, and daily tasks.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/u8yavr.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xv5duy.jpg)

This is a plaque inscribed by Mr. Mi Guangjiang for the Chinese Muslim Association of Canada. He has also visited this place himself.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/f360al.jpg)

I did not know President Bao Lin before I came to Toronto. I planned to visit the day before Eid al-Adha to check the time for the holiday prayers. I happened to run into the president, and he very warmly invited me to take a tour.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3jvut1.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jaa1ei.jpg)

To promote the Chinese Muslim Association, President Baolin installed a light box on the property window and kept it lit all night. This is how the building looks from the outside at night; it is very eye-catching.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yrsfg6.jpg)

The building management thought this was inappropriate and cut off the power without permission. The building is owned by Chinese people, but the overseas Chinese here do not seem friendly toward Muslims. President Baolin is now taking legal action to protect his rights.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6gd08m.jpg)

The third-floor property was just bought. When I arrived, it was being used for the first time, so many decorations had not been changed yet. The facilities look very simple, but everything necessary is there, except for an imam.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/rg27zu.jpg)

I was surprised that the Eid prayer was the next day, but they still had not found an imam to lead the prayer. President Baolin was anxious and made several calls, including asking a Pakistani student from a brother mosque for help, but he could not find anyone suitable. He said if there was no other choice, he would ask me to do it.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zo6goe.jpg)

I felt very caught off guard. In the Canadian Hui Muslim community, there are about a hundred people who visit regularly. Many people from China have a weak sense of faith and are not in this circle. Others did not have the chance to learn back home, so they have a strong desire to learn here. Everyone is eager for a full-time imam to guide them in their studies and daily worship, but unfortunately, they have not found the right person yet.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ozd4pb.jpg)

President Baolin really hopes that a capable and willing scholar will come here to teach, and he will provide all the help he can.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wqvi33.jpg)

Then, President Baolin drove me around to visit a few unique mosques in the area.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/byz31t.jpg)

JAME ABU BAKR SIDDIQUE

In North America, the largest and most numerous mosques are definitely built by our Pakistani brothers. This is one of them. It shows that the Pakistani community is doing well overseas, not just in North America but also in Europe. They are doing better than the Chinese community, which I will explain in more detail later.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pycsxz.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/15ytsr.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pnprh2.jpg)

Toronto Prayer Schedule

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/l2yojd.jpg)

Islamic Foundation of Toronto

The Islamic Foundation of Toronto is a mosque still managed by our Pakistani brothers. President Baolin came here to ask the mosque management to help find a Pakistani student to lead the Eid al-Adha (Qurban Festival) prayer. The Pakistani prayer time is June 17, while the Hui Muslim community's is on the 16th, but we could not find anyone available.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/u8r0lx.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mqunei.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ho1u85.jpg)

Islamic Institute of Toronto

The Islamic Institute of Toronto was founded in 1996 and moved to its new location in 2006. It covers 8 acres and is very large.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xnt76e.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1t4tx7.jpg)

The school is usually not open to the public, but I knocked on the door and went in for a visit.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nrhg93.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zypkvy.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lg8vz7.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/j0sorf.jpg)

Masjid Qurtabah

This is the home of the Muslim Association of Canada. President Baolin brought me here because before the Hui Muslim community bought their own property, they did not have a permanent place for religious activities, so they used to borrow this space for Chinese Muslim events.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1gkmlk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5y5vz6.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pighmj.jpg)

This place will also soon be renovated into a large Islamic activity center.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4fsrxy.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5r3uoo.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9018z2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2b4hqs.jpg)

This is a mosque for Somali people.

Seeing that the Somali community has its own mosque—which was converted from a church, as many mosques in Canada were—the president hopes that one day he can also establish a Chinese Muslim community center.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0x95sz.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/apmdgq.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gbafzg.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/xmkatk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/643jzn.jpg)

Oriental Palace (Dongfang Gong)

This is truly the Lanzhou chain brand Oriental Palace (Dongfang Gong). It has arrived in Canada and has many branches in Toronto.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4ktn2k.jpg)

This bowl of beef noodles costs 14.99 Canadian dollars, which is about 80 yuan. That does not include the tip. With a 12-15% tip, the bowl costs nearly 100 yuan. Prices in Canada are high. Even if you work locally and earn Canadian dollars, eating out is not cheap.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qhzppj.jpg)

I saw at least three Dongfanggong restaurants in Toronto.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vanpxz.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/43lwmc.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/o9ydcq.jpg)

To be fair, the noodles tasted quite good. They were chewy and had plenty of meat. This was the most satisfying meal I have had in my ten days in Canada. Thanks to Brother Baolin.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/z6ap4d.jpg)

But the lamb skewers (yangrouchuan) are still better back home.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vxknxq.jpg)

This smashed cucumber salad (pai huanggua) is more expensive than the lamb skewers. It is common in Western countries for meat to be cheaper than vegetables.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nnhfpi.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lvxh2n.jpg)

After finishing the beef noodles, Brother Baolin drove me back to the hotel to rest. I had flown all night from Calgary to Toronto and dealt with a two-hour time difference. I fell asleep as soon as I lay down in bed, needing to recharge for Eid al-Adha the next day.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yg0mqs.jpg)

Early the next morning, Brother Baolin was setting up the venue, and I arrived early too. Unfortunately, we still could not find an imam to lead the prayer. However, a Hui Muslim brother from Xinjiang who had studied the scriptures stepped up. Even though he had a cold, he insisted on leading everyone in the Eid prayer. I will not post his photo.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jonjsm.jpg)

After the prayer, I shared some thoughts on what I have learned from my travels over the years. I said the North American Chinese Muslim Association needs doers like Brother Baolin. Many people are well-read in scriptures but lack real-world experience. Their words and actions are disconnected from society, and they create division locally. Such 'smart' people do not help the faith. Throughout history, we have never lacked scholars who only talk. We need well-rounded, excellent people to strengthen our ability to take root and thrive in a foreign land.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pokc88.jpg)

After the sharing session, we went to the second floor to eat. Everyone brought food they prepared themselves, sharing their favorite hometown dishes. Those who did not have a specialty just contributed money.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m12stp.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k8whgz.jpg)

For Hui Muslims, a holiday is not complete without fried dough (youxiang). This youxiang looks like the Henan style. There are many Henan friends (dost) in the community, so much so that before I came here, people told me there was a Henan Hui neighborhood in Canada. In reality, it is not just Henan people here. There are people from Beijing, Inner Mongolia, the Northwest, and Uyghurs.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wod176.jpg)

The lamb soup (yangrou tang) and chive pockets (jiucai hezi) both have a Henan flair. They smelled delicious and were very good.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zui8jf.jpg)

Some non-Chinese brothers also attended the event. They met Chinese Muslims through marriage.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/78n3db.jpg)

As is the custom, Eid al-Adha (Qurban) ended in the morning. In the afternoon, Vice President Davei ZHU drove me to Toronto's most famous attraction, Niagara Falls, which often appears on the National Geographic channel.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/14mio2.jpg)

Rainbow Bridge

Rainbow Bridge connects Canada and the United States and was built by both countries. Driving across this bridge takes you to New York State in the U.S. Canadian citizens do not need a visa to enter the U.S., and the process is very simple, so many Canadians choose to work in the U.S.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nsco3l.jpg)

Niagara Falls spans both the U.S. and Canada, but the main view is on the Canadian side. You cannot see the full picture if you stand on the American side.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/l16c5y.jpg)

It took us over two hours to drive there, and we even hit traffic on the way. I chatted a lot with Vice President ZHU. His whole family has lived in Canada for over twenty years. As overseas Chinese, their longing for their hometown grows deeper with age.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wv4jp9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7muti9.jpg)

When the weather is good, you can see a double rainbow at the waterfall.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/r8bej0.jpg)

There is no admission fee for natural scenery like the waterfall, but you have to pay if you want to take a boat for a close-up tour or ride the zipline.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jvnp2c.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vr8wr5.jpg)

A group of Muslims were having a picnic on the grass. The proportion of Muslims in Canada is very high, and you can see young women wearing beautiful headscarves everywhere on the street. However, Vice President ZHU warned me not to walk barefoot on the grass. He said that the number of drug addicts in Canada has increased over the years, and some of them carelessly throw used needles into the grass.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cn2hmp.jpg)

There are many halal restaurants around the waterfall, and there is a whole street full of halal food. We stumbled upon this Indian buffet restaurant by chance.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vbsxtp.jpg)

The buffet is 21 Canadian dollars per person. The selection is quite complete, and this price is actually not expensive. Even in Beijing, a similar Indian-Pakistani buffet would cost 98 yuan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1w7qcu.jpg)

Indian-Pakistani cuisine is like Lanzhou hand-pulled noodles (Lanzhou lamian) in the eyes of Westerners; it is convenient, delicious, affordable, and accepted by people from all countries.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2lz4s5.jpg)

Near the big Ferris wheel is this halal food street. You do not have to worry about finding halal food in Canada at all; it is everywhere. However, halal food in North America mainly falls into three categories: Arab, Turkish, and Indo-Pakistani, and most of it is fast food or snacks.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9m5ufn.jpg)

This place serves Moroccan-style food.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/354ywx.jpg)

After saying goodbye to President Zhu, I left Toronto the next day. On the way to the airport, I saw this mosque converted from a church. Many places of worship in the city are usually locked, and this one was not open.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/th6w2h.jpg)

It is very common to see young women wearing headscarves in public. The streets of Canada are very diverse with people of all races. I even saw many women wearing headscarves working as customs and airport staff.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zii0bz.jpg)

On the way back, the president told me a story about someone he knew. When he first arrived in Canada, he lived with an international student from Beijing, and they later became good friends. This roommate was introverted, quiet, and grew up in a military compound, but he had a tense relationship with his family. After coming to Canada, he almost never contacted his family. He had a very small social circle and showed signs of depression. Later, they moved to different cities for work. They kept in touch occasionally but never met again. A few years ago, the president received a call from the Canadian police. The police said his friend was found dead in his car in a parking lot. They ruled out foul play, and it was likely an overdose. The only emergency contact number the friend had left was the president's, with no other family contact information.

Because the president was not a relative, the police could not provide more information. Later, the police buried him in a public cemetery as an unclaimed body. The president only knew his name was Zhang Qiang, he was from Beijing, and he was about 50 years old when he died. He was single, had no wife or children, and there was not even a single photo of him. He died alone in a foreign land, and his family still does not know where he is.

The president hoped I could find out about this person in Beijing, but there is too little useful information. I also feel that since his family does not know he has passed away, it might be better if they never find out. That way, his family can still hold onto the hope that he is alive somewhere in the world.

---

## Egypt Muslim Travel Guide: Cairo Museum, Luxor Restaurants and Real Travel Trap Warnings
Source: https://salaamalykum.com
Hash: a35caec8e89a71e16fe173c215dcca5a2f499fe674e1420ef89962526dd45da6

# Egypt Muslim Travel Guide: Cairo Museum, Luxor Restaurants and Real Travel Trap Warnings

Reposted from the web

Summary: This Egypt Muslim travel guide continues with ancient Egyptian history, the Egyptian Museum, Luxor, Nile-side dining, halal-friendly restaurant notes, local scams, travel traps, and reflections on Muslim life in Egypt.

A Guide to Avoiding Travel Traps in Egypt is presented here as a firsthand travel account in clear English, beginning with this scene: If you are not interested in Egyptian history, you will just be a casual tourist here. However, seeing the high level of ancient Egyptian civilization is an experience unmatched anywhere else in the world. The account keeps its focus on Quanzhou Muslims, Hui Muslims, Islamic History while preserving the names, places, food, and historical details from the Chinese source.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/n9qwmv.jpg)

If you are not interested in Egyptian history, you will just be a casual tourist here. However, seeing the high level of ancient Egyptian civilization is an experience unmatched anywhere else in the world.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zbl79z.jpg)

Some say ancient Egyptians and modern Egyptians are not the same race, but that is not a fact. Ancient Egyptians did not disappear. Through thousands of years of cultural exchange, they were assimilated, and modern Egyptians carry the genes of the ancient Egyptians.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bne9ik.jpg)

Although Egyptians are nominally divided into Arabs, Copts, or Nubians, their bloodlines are actually very similar. They all speak Arabic and have intermarried and assimilated so much that there is no such thing as a pure-blooded, independent ethnic group.

For example, the number of Copts in Egypt is decreasing every year because they are blending into the Arab population. When a Coptic Christian converts to Islam, by the third generation, they identify themselves as Arab.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ocvddo.jpg)

In the 7th century, Copts helped the Arabs conquer the Eastern Roman Empire. Because Copts were Miaphysites, the Eastern Roman Empire viewed them as heretics. Under Arab rule, the Copts actually lived better. Some say many Copts converted to Islam to pay fewer taxes, but there were not many tax benefits for converting, so there was no large-scale conversion of Copts in the early days.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/p2ij8t.jpg)

During the Fatimid Caliphate, because the rulers were Shia, they were even harsher toward Sunni Muslims than toward Coptic Christians. Copts and Jews were protected and thrived during this period. Even during the Ayyubid and Mamluk dynasties, Copts remained under protection. The Mamluks were former slaves who became rulers, so they empathized with the Copts.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0aky5b.jpg)

By the mid-14th century, whenever there were large-scale riots, many Copts converted to protect themselves. According to Egyptian law at the time, a Muslim's property could not be inherited by a non-Muslim. This led the descendants of converts to choose Islam to keep their property. It was from this period that Copts became a minority in Egypt.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ioqsb0.jpg)

Later, Muslim citizens accused the converted Copts of not being true converts because they did not go to the mosque for namaz on time. So, even though Muslims are the majority in Egypt today, the country is not completely Islamized.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bchf54.jpg)

The second floor of the Egyptian Museum has a Tutankhamun gallery. There is a funny story here: a travel blogger on Douyin posted a video saying they received the highest honor in Egypt and were allowed to film inside the gallery, which is usually off-limits for photography. I was stopped by a staff member when I tried to take photos with my phone and had to delete them under his supervision. The photo below was taken from outside the museum using my phone's telephoto lens.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mo7800.jpg)

However, after I handed 200 Egyptian pounds (about 40 RMB) to the staff member, I was allowed to take photos. That is how I got this picture of Tutankhamun's golden mask.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3d9a7w.jpg)

Tutankhamun's golden mask is said to be the most valuable artifact in the world and is priceless. But in Egypt, as long as you have a tip, everything is negotiable. That includes the blogger who claimed they filmed the pyramids while the site was cleared. I checked the time of their video, and it was after sunset, which is after the pyramids close. Paying money to get in and film is definitely not the highest honor.

After getting this photo to prove the Douyin blogger was bluffing, I lost interest in seeing the rest of the exhibits. In the evening, we went to the local Star City Mall to eat Thai food.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2h72th.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/gqihdn.jpg)

It is rare to find a prayer room in public places in Egypt, unlike in the UAE or Saudi Arabia where you see them everywhere. This mall actually has a prayer room, which is worth noting.

SABAI Thai Food

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sii10e.jpg)

This Thai restaurant is quite popular locally and attracts many Chinese diners. The owner seems to have a misunderstanding of East and Southeast Asia, mixing in Chinese and Japanese elements. Perhaps to Arabs, we all look the same, just as we might struggle to tell the difference between Egyptians, Moroccans, Tunisians, Saudis, Qataris, Emiratis, Lebanese, Yemenis, Syrians, Jordanians, Bahrainis, Libyans, and Algerians.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k3lrak.jpg)

You do not see halal signs in restaurants in Egypt, so you can just walk in anywhere. However, you should be careful with Chinese food and ask clearly. This shop marks its menu as halal because many Arabs assume Chinese food is not halal, and some restaurants also sell alcohol.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7f33u0.jpg)

The chef at this Thai place is Black, but the food is quite authentic. Sour and spicy Thai dishes are very appetizing in the hot Middle East. The portions are small, but the price is cheap, costing about 100 RMB per person.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jnxho2.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0xnpi8.jpg)

Tom Yum Goong soup

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ph9c34.jpg)

MORISUSHI Japanese food

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6gw1av.jpg)

I had a Japanese meal at a shopping center in New Cairo. After traveling in the Middle East for over ten days and eating Arabic food almost every day, it was nice to change things up.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/c2a0ah.jpg)

There is a halal sign on the soy sauce bottle, which is a new thing to see in an Arab country.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/r3rq2o.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9xd8zv.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/75wzwr.jpg)

Patchi chocolate shop

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g2rcdr.jpg)

This is a Lebanese chocolate brand, said to be the royal family's choice for desserts. It is sold very cheaply in Egypt, at about 300 RMB per kilogram.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0xq474.jpg)

Arabs love sweets, and there are many overweight people on the streets, which is really unhealthy. Eating too many sweets is also bad for children's brain development, so we should take this as a warning.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/1laztl.jpg)

Le Passage Cairo Hotel

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9bld7d.jpg)

We ate at the hotel restaurant most of the time. If you are just using Cairo as a transit stop and want to stay near the airport, I recommend this place. It is an 800-meter walk to the terminal, and the hotel also provides a free shuttle service.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/zyk87r.jpg)

At this hotel's buffet, you have to ask the staff to get the food for you, which feels a bit less free.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/hmsv40.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/4y2nod.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/r0s0j5.jpg)

We had steak and pasta at the hotel's Italian restaurant, and both were delicious. Friends, you can really choose a nice hotel for a vacation. If you do not want to go out, you can just stay by the hotel pool and soak up the sun, away from the noise outside.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cbwdcj.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/rzehnf.jpg)

Four Seasons Hotel

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/vsfpm4.jpg)

The Four Seasons in Cairo has newer facilities than the Ramses Hilton, but the buffet has fewer options.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3izcr4.jpg)

The hotel buffet costs less than 200 yuan, and drinks are charged separately.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m97ius.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/n2bivo.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bh1ybr.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/lakeej.jpg)

Outside the window is a view of the Nile. In Cairo, the most expensive rooms are the ones with a river view.

Luxor

Luxor is an ancient city in southern Egypt that was once the center of power in Egypt, similar to Xi'an in China. It only takes 50 minutes to fly from Cairo to Luxor. At the airport, the check-in staff ripped me off. He said I did not buy a child ticket for Fahim. I showed him my Trip.com order, but he still said he could not find the child ticket information. Since it was close to departure time, we paid him 1,000 Egyptian pounds (about 200 yuan) on the spot for the child ticket. Later, I contacted Trip.com and learned that my child ticket was used normally. This means the airport staff scammed me out of the price of a ticket, which is a very bad thing to do. I have asked Trip.com to investigate this with Cairo Airport, but I have not received an effective reply yet. Because it was a cash payment, unless they pull the surveillance footage from that time to prove the airport took my money, I have to swallow this loss.

Sonesta St. George Hotel Luxor

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/sv5dvt.jpg)

The climate in Luxor is much more comfortable than in Cairo. Cairo has serious pollution and is not suitable for long stays, but Luxor has fewer people and the pace of the whole city is much slower. You cannot use Uber in Luxor. You have to hail a taxi on the street. Always agree on the price before you get in and have plenty of small change ready. If you don't, the driver will keep whatever you give him and call it a tip.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6yheki.jpg)

Look at who has stayed at this hotel before. They play Chinese songs in the restaurant and have Chinese signs in the elevators, which feels very welcoming.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/p4lzz6.jpg)

For our first meal, we chose the hotel's Italian restaurant again. It has a clean, tidy European style, and the view of the Nile outside the window is stunning.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/v2uqs9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/2na0a6.jpg)

For breakfast, they serve honey straight from the honeycomb. It is natural and healthy.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8kxqvs.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9uhkts.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ovqbu0.jpg)

Famous sites in Luxor include the Valley of the Kings, Luxor Temple, Karnak Temple, the Colossi of Memnon, the Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut, and various pharaoh tombs. These are very attractive to archaeology fans, but for most tourists, it is just about taking photos. We booked a private one-day tour for two on Trip.com for 1,000 yuan per person. It included tickets, lunch, and a tour guide. The whole experience was pretty good.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/qcy1cp.jpg)

Colossi of Memnon

The statues represent the 18th Dynasty Pharaoh Amenhotep III. Both statues are of him. They are 18 meters tall and were built in 1350 BC, making them over 3,000 years old.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/mw67ql.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jo2kd8.jpg)

After a short stop at the statues, we went to the Valley of the Kings to see the tombs of Pharaoh Ramesses VI and Tutankhamun.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/59mnd1.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/bpu3g4.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/wgk3wo.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/txrl1w.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/jrhlb7.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5ub08l.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/67evqp.jpg)

The murals in the tomb of Ramesses VI still have the same colors they had 3,000 years ago.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/k8xov4.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/62v9so.jpg)

Tomb of Pharaoh Tutankhamun

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/tq5x6u.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ar3rzt.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/np41uk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/uf4t8q.jpg)

Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/329cfm.jpg)

The temple complex housing the pharaohs of past dynasties, along with the entire city of Luxor, was listed as a World Cultural Heritage site in 1979. This place was once used as a Christian monastery, and the reliefs unrelated to Christianity were removed.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/3w1i84.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/8uwpn9.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/5wweoe.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/m7s64l.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/7b8g0h.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/y1ntgs.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ed7qbh.jpg)

Karnak Temple

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/edpnkr.jpg)

The temple was built in 2000 BC, making it over four thousand years old, and it covers an area larger than half of Manhattan.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/410uo6.jpg)

Visiting the temple during the day is a test of physical strength because the sun is so strong. When Fahim walked up to this ram-headed sphinx, he took a liking to the pebbles on the ground and refused to move. I stayed with him to play with the stones. During this time, Fahim was surrounded by Egyptian girls who took photos with him and kissed him.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/apxkdt.jpg)

The Egyptian tour guide said he could watch the child for me so I could go inside to look around, but I said forget it. I would rather stay and play with stones with Fahim. I am actually not interested in these temples. After seeing them, I just think they were built impressively and are very spectacular, and that is all.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/pctwdh.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/dmc5dt.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/yfdzut.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/fnf2gb.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ptpqnk.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/829mfr.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/kcf3m0.jpg)

Luxor Temple

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/g9wp1h.jpg)

The last stop was Luxor Mosque, which is also a landmark of Luxor with over three thousand years of history. However, what attracted me here was the gongbei built on top of the mosque. At the entrance, the guide asked if I wanted to visit the mosque or the mosque. I said the mosque. He looked surprised and confirmed it with me again. I was a bit tired by then, so I told him I would skip the mosque and just see the mosque before heading back.

Abu Haggag Mosque and Gongbei

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6blmqw.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nl5hl6.jpg)

This gongbei can be considered the only one in the world built on top of the world's oldest mosque, and it is the only mosque in the world built on mosque ruins.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/me7x85.jpg)

This mosque dates back to the Fatimid dynasty and was built on the ruins of a church inside the mosque. It was completed around 1286 and contains the tomb of the Sufi mystic Yusuf Abu Haggag. Haggag was a scholar born in Baghdad in the 13th century. According to local legend, he captured Luxor from the Roman Queen Tarza.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/9oq4qm.jpg)

Luxor Mosque was converted into a church by the Copts in 395 AD and then into a mosque in 640 AD. Therefore, it can be said that this is the oldest religious building in the world still in use, with a history spanning over three thousand four hundred years.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/95cyu5.jpg)

Inside the gongbei, there are some believers visiting the graves, with more women than men.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/ttgjxb.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/j6yfx6.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0rqyq3.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/liikwf.jpg)

The mosque suffered a fire in 2007. When it was rebuilt, stones from the mosque were used, and you can see pharaonic hieroglyphs on the carvings of this pillar.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/eqqvhu.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/05eiaw.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/042i1z.jpg)

Looking out from the mosque railing, you can see the Luxor Mosque. The once arrogant pharaohs compared themselves to gods, but they never imagined that three thousand years later, their mosque would become a tourist attraction for people to glance at in passing.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/0xodd0.jpg)

Al Iman Mosque

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/nalpkn.jpg)

This is the closest mosque to my hotel. Fewer than 10 people attend the adhan (banda). In Arab countries, people go back to sleep after the adhan, and almost no shops are open on the street. Only a few small shops selling flatbread (da bing) are open, so it is best to eat breakfast at the hotel, or you will have nothing to eat outside.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/452hm5.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/v3jdqx.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/noibta.jpg)

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/x8neig.jpg)

As a convenient fast food in the Arab region, shawarma (sharama) is as popular as our meat burger (roujiamo); it is economical, affordable, and delicious.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/a4yvwd.jpg)

El-KABABGY RESTAURANT

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/i6a2b7.jpg)

This shop is the number one ranked restaurant in Luxor on the TripAdvisor app, and after eating there, I feel it truly deserves this ranking. This shop is only 1 kilometer away from our hotel on foot.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/cklaio.jpg)

The outdoor part of the restaurant is right on the Nile pier. In December, the temperature in Luxor is only 20 degrees, which is very cool.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/rf5pcr.jpg)

The grilled meat at this shop is extremely fragrant and clearly better than other restaurants we have eaten at. The per capita consumption is only about one hundred yuan. In China, at an Arab restaurant of the same level, two people would spend at least four or five hundred.

![image](https://files.catbox.moe/6jaeh4.jpg)

Tagine pot (tajiguo)
